zh: 笑是最好的良药。
en: Laughter is the best medicine.

zh: 我坐下来不是为了写一个有趣的故事。
en: I don't sit down to write a funny story.

zh: 我坐下来写的每一件事都注定是悲伤的。
en: Every single thing I sit down to write is meant to be sad.

zh: 没有笑声的一天就是被浪费的一天。
en: A day without laughter is a day wasted.

zh: 喜剧只是一种严肃的有趣方式。
en: Comedy is simply a funny way of being serious.

zh: 一位著名的艺术品收藏家在穿过城市时，注意到一只脏猫在商店前从一个碟子里舔牛奶。
en: A famous art collector was walking through a city when he noticed a dirty cat lapping milk from a saucer in front of a store.

zh: 他愣了一下。
en: He did a double take.

zh: 他注意到那个碟子是一件非常珍贵的古董，于是他随意走进商店，提出要买这只猫。
en: He noticed that the saucer was an antique and very valuable, so he walked casually into the store and offered to buy the cat.

zh: 店主回答说："对不起，但这只猫不卖。"
en: The owner replied, "I'm sorry, but the cat isn't for sale."

zh: 收藏家说："拜托，我需要一只胃口好、会抓老鼠的饿猫放在家里。"
en: The collector said, "Please, I need a hungry cat with a good appetite around the house to catch mice."

zh: "我愿意付你200美元买那只猫。"
en: "I'll pay you 200 dollars for that cat."

zh: 店主说"成交"，然后把猫递了过去。
en: The owner said "sold" and handed over the cat.

zh: 收藏家继续说："嘿，这200美元，我在想你能不能把那个旧碟子也搭上。"
en: The collector continued, "Hey, for the 200 dollars I wonder if you could throw in that old saucer."

zh: "这只猫已经习惯用它了，这也能省得我再去找个碟子。"
en: "The cat's used to it and it'll save me from having to get a dish."

zh: 店主说："对不起，朋友，但那是我的幸运碟。"
en: And the owner said, "Sorry my friend, but that's my lucky saucer."

zh: "这周到目前为止，我已经卖了68只猫了。"
en: "So far this week I've sold 68 cats."

zh: 夏洛克·福尔摩斯和华生医生去进行了一次露营旅行。
en: Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson went on a camping trip.

zh: 享用完丰盛的晚餐和一瓶酒后，他们便歇息过夜，睡着了。
en: After a good dinner and a bottle of wine, they retired for the night and went to sleep.

zh: 几小时后，福尔摩斯醒来，问他忠实的朋友："华生，抬头看看天空，告诉我你看到了什么。"
en: Some hours later Holmes woke up and asked his faithful friend, "Watson, look up at the sky and tell me what you see."

zh: "我看到数百万颗星星，福尔摩斯，"华生回答道。
en: "I see millions of stars, Holmes," replied Watson.

zh: "那你从中推断出什么？"
en: "And what do you deduce from that?"

zh: 华生想了一会儿。
en: Watson thought for a minute.

zh: "嗯，根据天文学的研究，它告诉我存在数百万个星系和可能数十亿颗行星。"
en: "Well, according to the studies on astronomy, it tells me that there are millions of galaxies and potentially billions of planets."

zh: "根据星星的位置，我观察到土星在狮子座。"
en: "According to the position of the stars, I observe that Saturn is in Leo."

zh: "根据科学的时间测量，我推断时间大概是三点一刻。"
en: "According to the scientific measurement of time, I deduce that the time is probably a quarter past three."

zh: "根据当前的大气状况，我预测明天我们会有一个美好的日子。"
en: "According to the current atmosphere, I forecast that we will have a beautiful day tomorrow."

zh: "那它告诉你什么了，福尔摩斯？"
en: "And what does it tell you, Holmes?"

zh: 福尔摩斯沉默了一会儿。
en: Holmes was silent for a minute.

zh: "华生，"他说，"你这个白痴。"
en: "Watson," he said, "you idiot."

zh: "有人偷了我们的帐篷。"
en: "Somebody has stolen our tent."

zh: 一个中年男子感到非常沮丧。
en: A middle-aged man was feeling very down.

zh: 对他来说，一切都不顺利。
en: Everything had been going wrong for him.

zh: 他在工作上遇到了问题，他的妻子也为了另一个男人离开了他。
en: He had had problems at work and his wife had left him for another man.

zh: 所以，在他感到抑郁一个多月后，他决定去看医生。
en: So, after he had been feeling depressed for over a month, he decided to go to the doctor.

zh: 他不得不在医生的诊室里等了似乎很久。
en: He had to wait for what seemed like ages in the doctor's surgery.

zh: 他旁边那个流血的男人紧张地看着手表；一个女人在剧烈地咳嗽和打喷嚏；还有一个婴儿在尖叫。
en: The bleeding man next to him was looking at his watch nervously; a woman was coughing and sneezing badly; and a baby was screaming.

zh: 最后，在他等了大约半小时后，他被叫进去咨询。
en: Finally, after he had been waiting for about half an hour, he was called in for his consultation.

zh: 他进来时，医生正在她的桌子上写一张便条。
en: The doctor was writing a note at her desk when he came in.

zh: “对不起，我刚刚完成一些事情。恐怕我们今天早上一直很忙。”
en: "Sorry, I'm just finishing something. I'm afraid we've been very busy this morning."

zh: 然后她转向那个男人。“那么，有什么问题？”她问道。
en: She then turned to the man. "So, what's the problem?" she asked.

zh: “嗯，我最近遇到了一点危机，你知道，很多问题，”男人回答道。
en: "Well, I've been having a bit of a crisis, you know, lots of problems," replied the man.

zh: “而且我一直在做很多工作。”
en: "And I've been doing a lot of work."

zh: “嗯，你看起来非常苍白。”
en: "Mmm, you're looking very pale."

zh: 医生开始给他做检查。
en: The doctor started to examine him.

zh: “嗯，一切运作正常，”她随后宣布。
en: "Well, everything is working OK," she announced afterwards.

zh: “你的血压有点高，呼吸也很重，但除此之外一切都好。”
en: "You have slightly high blood pressure and you're breathing quite heavily, but otherwise everything's fine."

zh: “那么我能做什么？”男人问道。
en: "So what can I do?" asked the man.

zh: “我很快要去旅行。我将在美国待三个月。下星期的这个时候，我将抵达纽约。”
en: "I'm going on a trip soon. I will be in the United States for three months. This time next week, I'll be arriving in New York."

zh: 医生想了一会儿。
en: The doctor thought for a while.

zh: “我认为你需要的是开怀大笑。那会对你有很大好处。”
en: "I think what you need is a good laugh. That would do you a lot of good."

zh: “一个马戏团正在城里表演。你为什么不去看看呢？”
en: "A circus is performing in town. Why don't you go to see it?"

zh: “我听说有一个很棒的小丑，他真的会让你笑。他的名字叫格罗克。”
en: "I hear there's an amazing clown who'll really make you laugh. His name's Grock."

zh: “我就是格罗克，”男人悲伤地回答。
en: "I am Grock," replied the man sadly.

zh: 女孩：为什么猫坐在电脑上？
en: Girl: Why was the cat sitting on the computer?

zh: 男孩：他在盯着鼠标。
en: Boy: He was keeping an eye on the mouse.

zh: 政治老师：谁是议长？
en: Teacher of politics: Who is the speaker of the House?

zh: 学生：教授，这次考试我已经尽力了。我真的认为我不应该得零分。
en: Student: Professor, I did the best I could on this test. I really don't think I deserve a zero.

zh: 教授：我也这么认为。但那是我能给的最低分数。
en: Professor: Neither do I. But that's the lowest grade I'm allowed to give.

zh: 老师：水的化学式是什么？
en: Teacher: What is the chemical formula for water?

zh: 老师：你在说什么？
en: Teacher: What are you talking about?

zh: 学生：昨天你说它是H到O！
en: Student: Yesterday you said it's H to O!

zh: 我们为什么需要幽默？
en: Why do we need humour?

zh: 例如，我认为幽默是与生俱来的。
en: Example I think humour is hard-wired into us.

zh: 憨豆先生是国际公认的电影和电视剧喜剧角色。
en: Mr Bean is an internationally recognised comedy character in films and TV series.

zh: 他以不断遭遇尴尬处境而闻名，这极大地娱乐了来自所有民族和文化背景的观众。
en: He has a reputation for constantly encountering awkward situations, which greatly amuses audiences of all nationalities and cultures.

zh: 他的幽默总是通过一系列简单而有趣的行为得以体现，这些行为完全依赖于通用的肢体语言。
en: His humour is always made clear through a series of simple and funny acts that rely purely on body language that is universal.

zh: 我最喜欢的片段之一是憨豆先生在一家高档餐厅。
en: One of my favourite episodes was Mr Bean in a fancy restaurant.

zh: 在餐桌旁就座后，憨豆先生拿出一张卡片，在上面写了几个字，把它放进一个信封里，然后放在桌子上。
en: After being seated at his table, Mr Bean takes out a card, writes a few words on it, puts it in an envelope and places it on the table.

zh: 过了一会儿，他又看向那个信封，但这次他看起来很惊讶，好像不知道它在那里。
en: After a moment, he looks back at the envelope but this time he looks surprised, as if he did not know it was there.

zh: 他打开它，发现是一张生日贺卡，非常高兴地把它放在桌子上让每个人都能看到。
en: He opens it to find a birthday card and very happily puts it on the table for everyone to see.

zh: 当他看菜单时，一种惊讶的表情迅速出现在他脸上。
en: When he looks at the menu, an astonished look quickly appears on his face.

zh: 他把所有的钱从钱包里拿出来，数了数，然后放进一个小碟子里。
en: He takes all the money out of his wallet, counts it and puts it in a saucer.

zh: 然后他关切地看看菜单，又看看钱，直到找到一样让他微笑的东西。
en: He then looks from the menu to the money with concern until he finds one thing that makes him smile.

zh: 然后他点了一道叫做“鞑靼牛排”的菜。
en: Then he orders a dish called "steak tartare".

zh: 菜上来时，他震惊地发现“鞑靼牛排”实际上是生汉堡肉。
en: the dish arrives, he is shocked to discover that "steak tartare" is actually raw hamburger.

zh: 他尝试着去吃它，但从他咀嚼时脸上的表情可以清楚地看出，他觉得这味道实在太糟糕了。
en: He makes an attempt to eat it, but it is clear from the look on his face as he is chewing that he finds the taste truly awful.

zh: 他无法隐藏自己的感受，除非服务员询问是否一切都好。
en: He cannot hide his feelings, except when the waiters ask if everything is all right.

zh: 当这种情况发生时，他微笑着点头，表示一切都好。
en: When this happens, he smiles and nods, indicating that everything is fine.

zh: 然而，当服务员不看的时候，他就忙着把生肉藏到他能够到的任何地方——糖碗、小花瓶、面包卷里和盘子下面。
en: When the waiters are not looking, however, he busies himself hiding the raw meat anywhere he can reach — the sugar bowl, the tiny flower vase, inside a bread roll and under a plate.

zh: 最后他变得如此绝望，甚至把一些肉藏在了坐在他附近的一位女士的钱包里，还把一些扔进了餐厅小提琴手的裤子里！
en: He becomes so desperate in the end that he even hides some inside the purse of a woman sitting near him and throws some down the trousers of the restaurant's violinist!

zh: 我喜欢在电视上看憨豆先生，但我不想在现实生活中遇到像他那样的人，而且我当然不想和他共进晚餐！
en: I like to watch Mr Bean on TV but I wouldn't want to meet someone like him in real life, and I certainly wouldn't want to have dinner with him!

zh: 罗温·阿特金森是一位英国演员、喜剧演员和编剧。
en: Rowan Atkinson is an English actor, comedian and screenwriter.

zh: 在英国，阿特金森先生主要以许多成功的电视节目而闻名，但在国际上，我们最熟悉的是他饰演的笨拙、滑稽但总是有趣的憨豆先生。
en: In Britain, Mr Atkinson is mostly famous for many successful TV shows but internationally we know him best as the awkward, clumsy, but always amusing Mr Bean.

zh: 如果你认为罗温·阿特金森和他著名的创作有任何相似之处，那么你会大吃一惊。
en: If you think that Rowan Atkinson is anything like his famous creation, then you are in for a surprise.

zh: 相反，他一点也不像憨豆先生。
en: On the contrary, he is nothing like Mr Bean.

zh: 罗温出生于1955年1月。
en: Rowan was born on January, 1955.

zh: 他在一个农场与父母和三个哥哥一起长大。
en: He grew up on a farm with his parents and three older brothers.

zh: 他的父亲非常严格，并且不相信电视的价值。
en: His father was very strict and did not believe in the value of television.

zh: 罗温的学业生涯非常成功。
en: Rowan had a very successful academic career.

zh: 13岁时，他获得了一所寄宿学校的奖学金。
en: At the age of 13, he was awarded a scholarship to a boarding school.

zh: 后来，他在纽卡斯尔大学学习，并在同年级取得了最高分。
en: Later, he studied at Newcastle University where he attained the highest marks in his year.

zh: 在牛津大学，他获得了电气工程硕士学位。
en: At Oxford, he obtained his master's degree in electrical engineering.

zh: 这也许可以解释为什么他热爱快车，他拥有很多辆。
en: This may explain why he loves fast cars, of which he has many.

zh: 罗温年轻时口吃，为了让自己的演讲更清晰，他开始非常小心地说话。
en: When Rowan was young, he had a stutter and to make his speech clearer, he began to speak very carefully.

zh: 这也许就是他的一些角色有不同寻常的说话方式的原因。
en: This may be why some of his characters have unusual ways of speaking.

zh: 早在小学时，罗温就已经表现出了表演天赋，但直到后来上大学时，他才决定成为一名演员。
en: As early as primary school, Rowan had already shown a talent for acting, but it was only later at university that he decided to become an actor.

zh: 一个关键事件发生在1976年的一天，当时他正在玩耍，对着镜子做鬼脸。
en: A key event occurred one day in 1976 when he was playing around and pulling a face in the mirror.

zh: “我发现了我的脸，”他后来说。
en: "I discovered my face," he said later.

zh: 曾为BBC电视台担任制片人的约翰·劳埃德说，很少有人能遇到这样的天才，当他与阿特金森合作时，他确信罗温会比查理·卓别林更出名。
en: John Lloyd, who worked as a producer for BBC Television, says that one rarely meets someone with such genius, and that when he worked with Atkinson he became convinced that Rowan would be more famous than Charlie Chaplin.

zh: 去年冬天的大雪导致希思罗机场长时间延误。
en: The heavy snow last winter caused long delays at Heathrow Airport.

zh: 一天下午，数百名不幸的乘客被告知他们原本预计飞往纽约的航班已被取消。
en: One afternoon, hundreds of unlucky passengers, who had been expecting to board a flight to New York, were told it had been cancelled.

zh: 一位不幸的客服人员正在处理所有乘客的问题，这时一位愤怒的男子，他已经排队了一段时间，挤到了前面。
en: One unfortunate agent was dealing with all the passengers, when an angry man, who had been standing in line for some time, pushed his way to the front.

zh: “我现在就要这趟航班的一个头等舱座位！”这名男子愤怒地要求道。
en: "I want a first class seat on this flight, now!" demanded the man angrily.

zh: 年轻的客服人员礼貌地回答：“对不起，先生，但我不能忽视这些人。
en: The young agent replied politely, "I'm sorry, sir, but I can't ignore these people.

zh: 如果您能排队等候，我会看看我能做些什么。”
en: If you could wait your turn, I'll see what I can do."

zh: 这并没有让该男子满意。
en: This did not please the man.

zh: 他对着年轻的客服人员尖叫，以便他身后排队的乘客都能听到：“你知道我是谁吗？”
en: He screamed at the young agent so that the passengers waiting in line behind him could hear, "DO YOU KNOW WHO I AM?"

zh: 客服人员笑了笑，站起来大声说道：“请注意！
en: The agent smiled, stood up and spoke loudly, "Attention, please!

zh: 我们这里有一位不知道自己是谁的乘客。
en: We have a passenger here WHO DOES NOT KNOW WHO HE IS.

zh: 如果有人能帮助他找到他的身份，请到登机口来。”
en: If anyone can help him find his identity, please come to the gate."

zh: 想象一顶圆顶礼帽、一撮小胡子和一根手杖。
en: Imagine a bowler hat, a moustache and a walking cane.

zh: 这可能就是塑造查理·卓别林作为“小流浪汉”形象的全部所需，这是世界上最著名的喜剧角色之一。
en: This is probably all it takes to make an image of Charlie Chaplin as the "Little Tramp", one of the most famous comedy characters in the world.

zh: 查尔斯·斯宾塞·卓别林于1889年4月16日出生于伦敦。
en: Charles Spencer Chaplin was born in London on 16 April, 1889.

zh: 他的父母都是成功的演员和歌手，所以他追随他们的脚步是很自然的。
en: Both his father and mother were successful actors and singers, so it was natural for him to follow in their footsteps.

zh: 然而，卓别林年仅10岁时，他的父亲就去世了，随后他的母亲也病倒了。
en: However, his father died when Chaplin was just 10 years old, and his mother then became sick.

zh: 突然间，查理和他的哥哥不得不独自谋生。
en: Suddenly, Charlie and his elder brother had to make a life on their own.

zh: 卓别林加入了一个青年舞台剧团。
en: Chaplin joined a youth stage group.

zh: 随后他开始在舞台剧中工作，并于1910年在美国巡回演出。
en: He then began working in stage shows and toured the US in 1910.

zh: 观众们很喜欢他，于是在1912年，他得到了一份电影合同。
en: The audiences loved him, and in 1912, he was offered a movie contract.

zh: 他的早期电影取得了巨大的商业成功，以至于电影公司都争先恐后地聘请他。
en: His early films were such huge commercial successes that film companies were falling over themselves to hire him.

zh: 为了对自己的工作有更大的控制权，卓别林于1917年在好莱坞成立了自己的工作室——联美公司。
en: In order to have greater control over his work, Chaplin set up his own studio — United Artists — in Hollywood in 1917.

zh: 卓别林自己编写、导演、主演并剪辑了他所有的电影，制作了一系列票房热门影片。
en: Chaplin wrote, directed, starred in and edited all his own films, producing a series of box office hits.

zh: 他最成功的作品可能是1936年的默片《摩登时代》。
en: His most successful one is probably the silent film Modern Times in 1936.

zh: 在电影中，卓别林的小流浪汉角色在一家工厂工作，遭受精神折磨，进了监狱，但最终成为了英雄！
en: In the film, Chaplin's Little Tramp character works in a factory, suffers mental torment, goes to jail, but finally becomes a hero!

zh: 除了肢体喜剧之外，这部电影还揭示了生产线上对工人的剥削。
en: Aside from the physical comedy, the film shines a light on the exploitation of workers on production lines.

zh: 卓别林的电影将闹剧喜剧提升到了一个新的水平。
en: Chaplin's films elevated slapstick comedy to a new level.

zh: 他的电影以精心设计的场景为特色。
en: His films featured carefully designed scenes.

zh: 卓别林的一些场景需要花费数天时间来规划和设计。
en: Some of Chaplin's scenes took days to plan and design.

zh: 这就是卓别林追求完美的方式。
en: Such was how Chaplin went after perfection.

zh: 让笑话看起来毫不费力且简单，这是他天才的一部分。
en: Making the joke look effortless and simple was part of his genius.

zh: 卓别林的电影远不止是一系列松散编织在一起的闹剧场景。
en: Chaplin's films were much more than just a series of slapstick scenes loosely woven together.

zh: 他抓住了弱势群体的处境——饥饿、贫穷、懒惰——并将其变成了喜剧的黄金。
en: He took the situation of the underdog — hunger, poverty, laziness — and made it into comedy gold.

zh: 在1950年的一次采访中，卓别林说道：“我听过对幽默最好的定义是，它让人们陷入麻烦又摆脱麻烦......
en: In an interview in 1950, Chaplin stated "The best definition of humour I ever heard is that it's getting people in and out of trouble ...

zh: 我对大多数事情都很感性，但对自己的工作却很客观。
en: I'm emotional about most things but objective about my work.

zh: 我从中得到的不是满足；而是解脱。”
en: I don't get satisfaction out of it; I get relief."

zh: 卓别林扮演了一个需要克服困难的普通人，并确保自己总能有一个圆满的结局。
en: Chaplin acted as an ordinary man who had difficulties to overcome, and made sure he always ended up smelling of roses.

zh: 卓别林有时被批评过于多愁善感，但今天的喜剧演员仍然认为他的电影对他们的表演方法具有影响力。
en: Chaplin is sometimes criticised for being overly sentimental, but comic actors of today still think his films are influential in their approach to acting.

zh: 卓别林会说所有这些话吗？
en: Would Chaplin have said all those words?

zh: 眉毛一扬，面无表情，再抬一下圆顶礼帽——有时一幅画面胜过千言万语。
en: A raise of the eyebrow, a deadpan expression and a lift of the bowler hat — sometimes a picture says a thousand words.

zh: 如果你在街上拦住任何一个人，请他们说出一个英国喜剧演员的名字，你很可能会发现他们能做到！
en: If you stopped any person on the street and asked them to name a British comedian, chances are you would find that they could!

zh: 英国喜剧在全世界都受欢迎且备受喜爱。
en: British comedy is popular and well-liked all over the world.

zh: 从查理·卓别林这样在电影默片时代闻名的演员，到在电视和电影中扮演憨豆先生的罗温·艾金森，英国喜剧从未过时。
en: From actors such as Charlie Chaplin, who was famous during the silent age of filmmaking to Rowan Atkinson who plays Mr Bean on TV and in films, British comedy has never gone out of fashion.

zh: 尽管英国有许多受欢迎的戏剧演员，但对于全球观众来说，英国喜剧和喜剧演员有着某种特别之处。
en: And while there are many popular dramatic actors in Britain, there is something extra special about British comedy and comedians for global audiences.

zh: 英国喜剧成功和受欢迎的原因尤其值得探讨。
en: The reasons for the success and popularity of British comedy in particular, are interesting to explore.

zh: 幽默是每种文化的一部分，但可以说它是英国文化的核心特征之一。
en: Humour is a part of every culture but it could be said that it is one of the core characteristics of British culture.

zh: 大多数非英国人认为英国人非常保守、礼貌和正式，尽管这在某种程度上是事实，但英国人也喜欢通过幽默来表达自己。
en: Most non-British people see the British as being very reserved, polite and formal and while that is true to a certain degree, British people love to express themselves through humour too.

zh: 幽默可以作为一种方式，让更保守的人表达他们对生活、日常事务甚至其他人的挫折感！
en: Humour can act as a way for more reserved people to express their frustrations with life, daily routines and even other people!

zh: 英国人期望每个人都有幽默感，这很大程度上是日常生活的一部分。
en: British people expect everyone to have a sense of humour and it's very much a part of daily life.

zh: 你经常会发现上公交车的乘客和司机开玩笑，医生开些轻松的玩笑来缓解病人的忧虑，商人在演示中开玩笑以吸引听众的注意力。
en: You will often find passengers getting on buses joking with drivers, doctors making light hearted jokes to ease their patients' worries and business people making jokes in presentations to get their audiences' attention.

zh: 幽默有助于建立关系、放松情绪和发展友谊。
en: Humour helps to build relationships, relax moods and develop friendships.

zh: 那么，既然我们已经确定了幽默对英国人有多重要，到底是什么让他们发笑呢？
en: So now that we have established how important humour is to British people, what exactly makes them laugh?

zh: 英国幽默对其他文化来说有意义吗？
en: And does British humour make sense to other cultures?

zh: 笑话往往与文化相关。
en: Jokes are often related to culture.

zh: 一个例子就是英国的阶级制度。
en: One example would be the British class system.

zh: 有许多受欢迎的喜剧电视节目探讨了钱和地位较少的人试图模仿富人生活方式的荒谬性，还有一些节目幽默地探讨了生活较不富裕的人的生活。
en: There have been many popular comedy TV programmes which have explored the absurdity of people with less money and status attempting to copy the lifestyles of the wealthy and others which humorously explore life for the less well off.

zh: 对于不了解该文化的非英国人来说，可能很难完全理解那种幽默。
en: It would be difficult perhaps, for non-British people who don't know the culture to fully understand that kind of humour.

zh: 当谈到英国喜剧时，外国人可能难以理解的另一个领域是“文字游戏”。
en: Another area that might be difficult for foreigners to get when it comes to British comedy is "word play".

zh: 英国喜剧经常涉及具有双重含义的词语，或者发音相同但含义不同的词语。
en: British comedy often involve words that have two meanings, or words that sound the same but have different meanings.

zh: 英语中有很多这样的词，所以英国人真的很喜欢这种幽默。
en: There are lots of these in English so British people really enjoy this kind of humour.

zh: 例如，这里有一个来自 20 世纪 60 年代喜剧演员汤米·库珀的经典笑话：
en: For example, here is a classic joke from a comedian of the 1960s, Tommy Cooper:

zh: 我对健身教练说：“你能教我劈叉吗？”他说：“你的灵活性怎么样？”
en: I said to the Gym instructor, "Can you teach me to do the splits?" He said, "How flexible are you?"

zh: 我说：“我星期二没空。”
en: I said, "I can't make Tuesdays."

zh: 病人：“医生，医生，请帮帮我！我变得越来越矮了。”
en: Patient: "Doctor, Doctor, help me please! I'm getting shorter and shorter."

zh: 医生：“就在那儿等一会儿，耐心一点。”
en: Doctor: "just wait there and be a little patient."

zh: 这两个笑话都依赖于词语的双重含义，“flexible”（能够很好地移动身体或有空余时间表）和“patient”（愿意等待的人或看医生的人）。
en: Both of these jokes are dependent on double meanings of the words, "flexible" (being able to move your body very well or having an open schedule) and "patient" (someone willing to wait or someone seeing a doctor).

zh: 有时，不仅仅是单词有双重含义，表达方式也是如此。
en: Sometimes, it's not just words that having two meanings but also expressions.

zh: 以这个笑话为例：
en: Take this joke for example:

zh: 昨晚睡得像个木头......醒来时在壁炉里。
en: Slept like a log last night ... Woke up in the fireplace.

zh: “睡得像个木头”是英语中的一个习语，意思是睡得非常沉，你就像木头一样坚固且一动不动。
en: To sleep like a log is an idiom in English that means to sleep so deeply you seem as solid and unmoving as wood.

zh: 这个笑话通过“醒来时在壁炉里”将“睡得像个木头”变成了字面意思，因为壁炉是烧木头的地方。
en: This joke turns "slept like a log" into a literal meaning with "woke up in the fireplace" where one would burn logs.

zh: 现在，你是否觉得这类笑话有趣是个人喜好，但它们能被所有年龄段的英语使用者轻易理解，这一事实意味着它是英国喜剧的重要组成部分。
en: Now, whether or not you find these types of jokes funny is personal to you but the fact they are easily understood by English speakers of all ages, means that it is very much a part of British comedy.

zh: 英国喜剧另外两个独特的特点是开玩笑谈论在其他文化中被视为“禁忌”的话题，以及专注于自贬的幽默。
en: Two other characteristics of British comedy that are unique are joking about topics that would be considered "taboo" in other cultures and humour focused on self-depreciation.

zh: 例如，一个“禁忌”话题可能是政治和政治家，这是英国人喜欢开玩笑的事情。
en: For example, a "taboo" topic might be politics and politicians, which are something British people love to joke about.

zh: 人们可能会温和地嘲笑当权者“我的老板连洗澡都搞不定，更别说管理这家公司了！”，或者自嘲，例如“我正在实行那种新的‘海鲜’饮食法。
en: People might gently mock people in positions of power "my boss can't run a bath, let alone this company!" or mocking oneself, for example "I'm on that new 'seafood' diet.

zh: 如果我看到食物，我就吃！”
en: If I see food, I eat it!"

zh: 也许最普遍吸引人的英国幽默类型是罗温·艾金森、查理·卓别林等人使用的视觉、肢体喜剧。
en: Perhaps the most universally appealing type of British humour is the visual, physical type of comedy used by Rowan Atkinson, Charlie Chaplin and others.

zh: 他们在最简单、日常的情境中找到幽默，而我们在观看时往往会感到一种恐惧、尴尬和喜悦的混合情绪！
en: They find humour in the most simple, everyday situations and we tend to feel a mix of horror, embarrassment and delight watching them!

zh: 当然，对一个人有趣的事情对另一个人可能并不有趣。
en: Of course, what's funny for one person might not be for another.

zh: 一个人可能对一个笑话轻轻窃笑，而另一个人可能会笑得前仰后合。
en: While one person might lightly giggle at a joke, another might fall about with laugher.

zh: 但是英国喜剧演员和喜剧作家使用的广泛技巧，从讽刺，到双关语，到自嘲，意味着喜剧中有适合各种口味的东西。
en: But the wide range of techniques used by British comedians and comedy writers, from satire, to puns, to self-mockery, means that there is something for every taste in comedy.

zh: 尽管英国人讲笑话时礼貌的方式意味着有时甚至很难分辨那是不是一个玩笑！
en: Although the polite British approach to delivering jokes means that sometimes it's difficult to even tell if it is a joke or not!

zh: 对于学生来说，学习多个小时和准备考试真的会对我们的幸福和健康产生影响。
en: For students, studying for many hours and preparing for exams can really have an influence on our well-being and health.

zh: 虽然学业成功很重要，但我们需要保护自己免受压力的负面影响。
en: Although academic success is important, we need to protect ourselves from the negative effects of stress.

zh: 做到这一点的最好方法就是笑！
en: The best way to do that is through laughter!

zh: 想想当你情绪低落时，有人给你讲了个笑话或者你看到一些有趣的事情发生——通过笑这个简单的行为，你的情绪立刻得到了改善！
en: Just think about when you feel down and someone tells you a joke or you see something funny happening — immediately by the simple act of laughing your mood is improved!

zh: 笑对我们积极的心理学影响已经被科学家们测量过。
en: Laughter's positive psychological effects on us have been measured by scientists.

zh: 他们发现，当我们感到压力时，笑可以缓解紧张。
en: They have found that laughter can relieve tension when we feel stressed.

zh: 当我们感到焦虑或担心时，它也能帮助我们感到更放松和舒适。
en: It can also help us to feel more relaxed and comfortable when we are feeling anxious or worried.

zh: 即使你已经学习了很长时间，感到困倦和无聊，笑也能让你感到精力充沛，就像你刚小睡了一会儿一样！
en: Even if you have been studying for a long time and feeling sleepy and bored, laughing can make you feel energised as if you had just had a nap!

zh: 笑的好处如此之多，我希望你们都尝试找到一种方法，为你们的日常生活增添更多的乐趣和欢笑。
en: There are so many benefits of laughter that I hope you all try and find a way to add more fun and laughter to your daily life.

zh: 如果你能增加一天中微笑和大笑的次数，我保证你会提高你的生活质量。
en: If you can increase the number of times you smile and laugh in a day, I guarantee that you will enhance the quality of your life.

zh: 一个男人正住在一家酒店里。
en: A man is staying in a hotel.

zh: 他正在和一个朋友打电话，告诉她他7点在酒吧喝酒，并请她来那里找他。
en: He is talking to a friend and tells her he is having a drink in the bar at 7 and asks her to join him there.

zh: 然后，他去了酒吧，点了一杯橙汁，这时他听到一个声音：“那条领带真不错。”
en: Then, he goes to the bar and orders an orange juice when he hears a voice, "That's a nice tie."

zh: 男人环顾四周，但附近没有人。
en: The man looks around but nobody is near him.

zh: 然后他又听到了那个声音：“衬衫也不错。”
en: Then he hears the voice again, "Nice shirt, too."

zh: 男人以为可能是酒保在和他说话，但酒保并没有。
en: The man thinks that maybe the barman is talking to him, but the barman is not.

zh: 突然，那个男人又听到了那个声音：“你的夹克真的很时髦。”
en: Suddenly the man hears the voice again, "Your jacket is really smart."

zh: 知道他的朋友很快就会到，男人想知道是不是她，但周围没有他认识的人。
en: Knowing that his friend will arrive soon, the man wonders if it is her, but there is no one he recognises around.

zh: 男人不明白声音是从哪里来的，于是他向酒保询问。
en: The man can't understand where the voice is coming from, so he asks the barman about it.

zh: “打扰一下。你是在跟我说话吗？”
en: "Excuse me. Are you talking to me?"

zh: “不，先生，”酒保说。
en: "No, sir," says the barman.

zh: “那会是免费的 peanuts（花生）。”
en: "That'll be the complimentary peanuts."

zh: 昨天我们开车去学校时发生了一件有趣的事。
en: A funny thing happened as we were driving to school yesterday.

zh: 我们停在一个红灯前，正等着它变绿，这时一辆非常小的车开到了我们旁边的车道上。
en: We stopped at a red traffic light and were waiting for it to change to green when a very small car drove up in the lane next to us.

zh: 司机踩下刹车想停下来，但就在他这样做的时候，车开始翻滚。
en: The driver put his foot on the brake to stop but as he did so the car started to roll.

zh: 它非常轻柔、非常缓慢地侧翻了过去。
en: It rolled over very gently and very slowly right onto its side.

zh: 车里的男人坐在那里，看起来非常惊讶。
en: The man in the car sat there looking mighty surprised.

zh: 幸运的是，有一些人在红绿灯处等着过马路，所以他们迅速跑到车旁，检查司机是否安好，然后把车推回了轮子上。
en: Fortunately there were some people waiting to cross at the traffic light, so they quickly ran over to the car and checked that the driver was fine, and then pushed the car back onto its wheels.

zh: 当信号灯再次变回红色时，我们仍然停在红绿灯前——我们惊讶得张大了嘴巴！
en: We were still stopped at the traffic light when it changed back to red again — our mouths wide open in amazement!

zh: 教育是你可以用来改变世界的最强大的武器。
en: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.

zh: 我们应该建立一个全面培养学生德智体美劳全面发展的教育体系。
en: We should build an education system which comprehensively fosters the students' all-round moral, intellectual, physical and aesthetic grounding with a hard-working spirit.

zh: 我听见，我忘记。
en: I hear and I forget.

zh: 我看见，我记住。
en: I see and I remember.

zh: 我做，我理解。
en: I do and I understand.

zh: 只教育头脑而不教育心灵根本算不上教育。
en: Educating the mind without educating the heart is no education at all.

zh: 海伦·凯勒是一个极其特别的女孩。
en: Helen Keller was an exceptionally special girl.

zh: 到她七岁的时候，她仍然不会说话、阅读或写字，需要别人为她做所有事情。
en: By the time she was seven years old, she still couldn't speak, read or write and needed to have everything done for her.

zh: 这是因为她看不见也听不见。
en: This was because she couldn't see or hear.

zh: 由于这些严重的沟通限制，海伦的行为常常令人难以忍受。
en: With these severe restrictions to her communication, Helen's behaviour was often unbearable.

zh: 她有时很麻烦、固执和愤怒，并且当没有人理解她时，她有打碎东西的倾向。
en: She was sometimes troublesome, stubborn and angry, and had a tendency to break things when no one understood her.

zh: 海伦的父母得到了一位推荐给他们的优秀老师，一位名叫安妮·沙利文的女士。
en: Helen's parents got a superb teacher, recommended to them, a woman named Anne Sullivan.

zh: 安妮是波士顿一所盲人学校的老师和前学生。
en: Anne was a teacher and former student at a school for the blind in Boston.

zh: 她早年也有视力问题，所以她能理解海伦的困难。
en: She'd had eyesight problems early in life as well so she could relate to Helen's difficulties.

zh: 安妮教海伦语言的技术简单而直接。
en: Anne's technique to teach Helen language was simple and straightforward.

zh: 安妮通过在海伦手上写字来拼写单词，这样海伦就能在脑海中形成单词的图像。
en: Anne spelt words by writing on Helen's hand so that Helen could get a mental picture of the words.

zh: 她从“娃娃”这个词开始。
en: She started with the word "doll".

zh: 她会让海伦玩娃娃，然后在她手上拼写字母“D-O-L-L”。
en: She would let Helen play with the doll, and then spell the letters "D-O-L-L" on her hand.

zh: 当她们最初这样做时，海伦以为这是一个游戏。
en: When they initially did this, Helen thought it was a game.

zh: 她后来在她的书《我的一生》中写到了她的兴奋：
en: She wrote about her excitement later in her book, The Story of My Life:

zh: “我跑下楼去找我的母亲，我举起我的手并做出了‘娃娃’的字母。
en: "Running downstairs to my mother, | held up my hand and made the letters for 'doll'.

zh: 我不知道我是在拼写一个单词，甚至不知道单词的存在；我只是让我的手指像猴子一样模仿。”
en: | did not know that | was spelling a word or even that words existed; | was simply making my fingers go in monkey-like imitation."

zh: 一天，当她们在井边时，安妮把海伦的一只手放在水流下。
en: When they were at the well one day, Anne put one of Helen's hands under the stream of water.

zh: 当水流过海伦的手时，安妮在海伦的另一只手上拼写出“W-A-T-E-R”。
en: As the water flowed over Helen's hand, Anne spelt out "W-A-T-E-R" into Helen's other hand.

zh: 当安妮在海伦手上写字时，海伦突然意识到手指的运动意味着那凉爽的液体。
en: As Anne wrote on Helen's hand, Helen suddenly realised that the movement of the fingers meant the cool liquid.

zh: 这宝贵的知识给了她希望和快乐。
en: This precious knowledge gave her hope and joy.

zh: 终于，她为自己打开了文字的世界。
en: Finally, she got the world of words opened up to her.

zh: 既然海伦掌握了语言的关键，她便非常渴望学习更多并尽可能多地使用它。
en: Now that Helen grasped the key to language, she was very eager to learn more and use it as much as she could.

zh: 能看能听的孩子学习语言很容易，但对海伦来说，这是一个渐进的、有时甚至是痛苦的过程。
en: Children who can see and hear learn language easily, but for Helen, it was a gradual and sometimes painful process.

zh: 然而，因为海伦对学习如此热情，结果令人惊叹。
en: However, because Helen was so enthusiastic about learning, the results were amazing.

zh: 随着海伦知识和词汇量的增长，她问的问题越来越多。
en: As Helen's knowledge and vocabulary expanded, she asked more and more questions.

zh: 这很快引导她发现了更复杂的词汇，并改变了她的思维过程。
en: This soon led her to discover more complex words and changed her thinking process.

zh: 尝试学习‘爱’这个词是她记忆犹新的一次经历。
en: Trying to learn the word "love" was an experience that she remembered well.

zh: 这是她在书中描述的方式：
en: This is how she described it in her book:

zh: “我记得我第一次问‘爱’这个词的意思的那个早晨。
en: "I remember the morning that I first asked the meaning of the word 'love'.

zh: 那是在我认识很多词之前。
en: This was before I knew many words.

zh: 我在花园里发现了一些早开的紫罗兰，并把它们带给我的老师......
en: I had found a few early violets in the garden and brought them to my teacher ...

zh: 沙利文小姐温柔地搂着我，在我手上写下‘爱海伦’。
en: Miss Sullivan put her arm gently round me and wrote on my hand, ' love Helen.'

zh: ‘爱是什么？’我问道。
en: What is love?' I asked.

zh: 她把我拉近，指着我的心脏说：‘它在这里，’......
en: She drew me closer to her and said, 'It is here,' pointing to my heart ...

zh: 她的话让我非常困惑，因为那时除非我能触摸到，否则我无法理解任何东西。”
en: Her words puzzled me very much because I did not then understand anything unless I touched it."

zh: 尽管爱的含义对海伦来说仍然不明显，但她继续努力去理解它。
en: Although the meaning of love was still not apparent to Helen, she kept on trying to understand it.

zh: “我闻了闻她手中的紫罗兰，半用言语半用手势地问道，意思是‘爱是花的甜美吗？’
en: "I smelt the violets in her hand and asked, half in words, half in signs, a question which meant, 'Is love the sweetness of flowers?'

zh: 一天，当海伦和安妮在外面时，海伦感受到了阳光的温暖。
en: When Helen and Anne were outside one day, Helen felt the warmth of the sun's rays.

zh: 她向上指着，问那是不是爱。
en: She pointed up and asked if that was love.

zh: 当她的老师说那不是时，她感到困惑和失望。
en: When her teacher said that it wasn't, she was confused and disappointed.

zh: “我觉得很奇怪，我的老师不能把爱展示给我看。”
en: "I thought it strange that my teacher could not show me love."

zh: ‘思考’这个词对海伦来说也很难，因为它是如此抽象的概念。
en: The word "think" was also a difficult one for Helen because it is such an abstract concept.

zh: 然而，有一天当她正在完成一项简单的任务时，她有了一个突破。
en: However, one day as she was working on a simple task, she had a breakthrough.

zh: 她在安妮的帮助下制作项链时，注意到自己犯了一些错误。
en: She was making necklaces with the help of Anne when she noticed that she had made some mistakes.

zh: 因为她不确定如何改正，她停下来仔细思考。
en: Because she was uncertain how to fix them, she stopped to think carefully.

zh: 当她这样做时，安妮摸了摸海伦的额头，并在她手上写下了‘思考’这个词。
en: As she did this, Anne touched Helen's forehead and wrote the word "think" on her hand.

zh: “刹那间我明白了，这个词就是我头脑中正在进行的那个过程的名字。”
en: "In a flash I knew that the word was the name of the process that was going on in my head."

zh: 这是海伦第一次理解这样一个复杂的词——一个她无法触摸的东西的词。
en: It was the first time Helen had understood such a complex word — a word for something she couldn't touch.

zh: 在那一刻，她的思绪回到了‘爱’这个词上。
en: At that moment, her mind returned to the word "love".

zh: 当她再次思考它的含义时，太阳出来了。
en: As she thought about its meaning again, the sun came out.

zh: 她指着太阳，再次问她的老师那是不是爱。
en: She pointed to the sun and asked her teacher again if that was love.

zh: 安妮向海伦解释说，爱在某种程度上就像太阳和云彩。
en: Anne answered Helen by explaining that love was like the sun and clouds in a way.

zh: “你知道，你无法触摸云彩；但你能感觉到雨......
en: "You cannot touch the clouds, you know; but you feel the rain...

zh: 你也无法触摸爱；但你能感觉到它倾注于万物之中的甜蜜。
en: You cannot touch love either; but you feel the sweetness that it pours into everything.

zh: 没有爱，你就不会快乐，也不想玩耍。”
en: Without love you would not be happy or want to play."

zh: 在那个生动的时刻，海伦终于理解了‘爱’这个词的美丽真谛。
en: In that vivid moment, Helen finally understood the beautiful truth of the word "love".

zh: 现在我们如何获取知识和理解似乎很明显。
en: It seems obvious now how we acquire knowledge and understanding.

zh: 首先，我们需要问题。
en: To start with, we need questions.

zh: 然后，为了找到答案，我们观察周围的世界并研究事实。
en: Then, to find answers, we observe the world around us and study the facts.

zh: 之后，我们考虑可能的答案并逐一测试以找到正确的答案。
en: After that, we consider possible answers and test each to find the right ones.

zh: 尽管今天我们更习惯于在搜索引擎中输入几个关键词，等待互联网给我们答案，但现代科学家和思想家仍然在用这种分析方式解决世界问题——这对我们来说是幸运的。
en: Although today we are more used to typing a few key words into a search engine and waiting for the Internet to give us an answer, modern scientists and thinkers are still solving the world's problems with this type of analysis — luckily for us.

zh: 然而，在17世纪，当弗朗西斯·培根（1561-1626）提出这种思维方式是获取知识的途径时，他违背了当时的观点。
en: However, in the 17th century when Francis Bacon (1561-1626) suggested that this type of thinking was the way to gain knowledge, he was going against the views of the day.

zh: 尽管培根在詹姆斯国王的英国皇家宫廷中担任重要职位，但他真正的兴趣不是政府日常、缓慢且低效的工作方式，而是对知识的有价值的探索。
en: Although Bacon held an important rank in King James' royal court of England, his true interest was not the day-to-day, slow and inefficient working style of the government, but the worthy search for knowledge.

zh: 这当然不是他那个时代大多数人的兴趣所在。
en: This was certainly not the interest of most people in his days.

zh: 那时，人们更相信教会而不是事实，像伽利略·伽利莱（1564-1642）这样证明了“地球不是宇宙中心”观点的人，常常受到教会的惩罚，没有人站出来为他们辩护。
en: At that time, people believed more in the church than in facts, and people like Galileo Galilei (1564-1642), who proved the idea that "the Earth is not the centre of the universe", were often punished by the church with no one coming to their defence.

zh: 教会和许多人往往忽视事实，不想挑战他们一直舒适地相信的东西。
en: The church and many people tended to ignore the facts and didn't want to challenge what they had always comfortably believed.

zh: 事实上，当伽利略证明地球不是宇宙的中心时，人们非但没有相信他，反而选择了相信那些已有近2000年历史的观点！
en: In fact, when Galilei proved that the Earth was not the centre of the universe, instead of believing him, people chose to believe views that were almost 2,000 years old!

zh: 人们愿意相信这些原始的观点并不奇怪，因为它们是由伟大的哲学家亚里士多德（公元前384-322年）提出的。
en: It is not surprising that people wanted to believe these primitive ideas as they had been put forward by the great philosopher Aristotle (384-322 BCE).

zh: 他说地球一定是宇宙的中心，因为感觉地球是静止不动的。
en: He said that the Earth must be the centre of the universe because it felt like the Earth was standing still.

zh: 起初，人们赞同他的研究并敦促他继续，但后来当他证明亚里士多德错了时，他们变得愤怒并把他关进了监狱。
en: At first, people approved of his studies and urged him to continue, but later when he proved Aristotle wrong, they grew angry and put him in prison.

zh: 他们不想放弃他们一直认为是真理的东西。
en: They didn't want to abandon what they'd always thought as true.

zh: 而这种情况在今天仍然经常发生。
en: And this is still often true today.

zh: 人们会假设，如果某个重要且受人尊敬的人说某件事是对的，那么它一定就是对的。
en: People make the assumption that if someone important and respected says that something is right, then it must be so.

zh: 但尽管亚里士多德是一位伟人，激励了后世许多伟大的科学家和哲学家，他有时也会犯错。
en: But even though Aristotle was a great man who inspired many great scientists and philosophers after him, he was wrong at times.

zh: 而伽利略也犯过错误。
en: And Galilei also made mistakes.

zh: 他现在被称为天文学之父，但他认为地球是绕太阳做完美的圆周运动的。
en: He is now known as the father of astronomy but he believed that the Earth moved round the sun in a perfect circle.

zh: 因此，我们对周围世界的理解在不断增长和变化。
en: Therefore, our understanding of the world around us is constantly growing and changing.

zh: 换句话说，我们每天都在学习更多，我们中没有人可以坐享其成地说：“我们什么都知道。”
en: In other words, we learn more every day and none of us can ever sit back and say, "We know it all."

zh: 我们需要感谢过去的伟人们的智慧。
en: We need to thank the great men of the past for their wisdom.

zh: 他们明白我们并非无所不知，而且可能永远也不会，因为这将意味着一个充满问题的世界。
en: They understood that we don't know everything and probably never will, as this would mean a world with questions.

zh: 我们的许多知识和对世界的理解要归功于像培根和伽利略这样的人，他们足够勇敢，从传统思想的阴影中走出来，去寻找今天文明所建立的知识王国。
en: We owe so much of our knowledge and understanding of the world to people like Bacon and Galilei, who were brave enough to step out from the shadows of conventional thought in order to find the kingdom of knowledge that today's civilisation is built upon.

zh: 这些人知道知识和理解是值得为之奋斗的东西；有些人可能会说，它们对一个人来说比世界上所有的钱都更重要，对人类也更有益。
en: These men knew that knowledge and understanding are things to fight for; more vital to a man, and more beneficial to mankind, some might say, than all the money in the world.

zh: 所有的真理一旦被发现就很容易理解；关键在于发现它们。
en: All truths are easy to understand once they are discovered; the point is to discover them.

zh: 当我第一眼看这幅画时，它似乎只是一幅描绘花园里一个男人的相当简单的图画。
en: When I first look at this picture, it seems to be just a pretty simple drawing of a man in a garden.

zh: 然而，当更仔细地观察它时，我看到了一些有趣的东西。
en: However, when looking at it more closely, I see something interesting.

zh: 在画中，一个男人戴着一顶帽子和一条围裙，并且因为雨下得很大而拿着一把伞。
en: In the picture, a man is wearing a hat and an apron, and is carrying an umbrella because it is raining heavily.

zh: 他背对着我们。
en: He has his back turned to us.

zh: 我们可以看到他在他的花园里，因为他正用一个喷壶给花浇水。
en: We can see he is in his garden, for he is watering the flowers with a watering can.

zh: 这幅画的标题是“该给花浇水了”。
en: The drawing is entitled "It's Time to Water the Flowers".

zh: 这意味着这个男人显然热爱常规（和他的花），并且可能每天都在同一时间给他的花浇水。
en: This implies that this man obviously loves routines (and his flowers), and probably waters his flowers every day at the same time.

zh: 即使雨下得很大，他仍然这样做。
en: He is doing this even though it is raining heavily.

zh: 然而，植物并不需要他提供的额外水分。
en: However, the plants don't need the extra water he is providing.

zh: 他没有看到显而易见的事实，而只是不加思考地例行公事。
en: He is not seeing the obvious, but is just going through the motions without thinking.

zh: 我们可以从这幅画中得到的总体信息是，拥有常规和时间表很重要，因为它们可以帮助我们保持条理，但有时常规会控制我们，而不是我们控制常规。
en: The overall message we can take from this picture is that it is important to have routines and timetables, as they can help us stay organised, but sometimes routines can control us, rather than us controlling the routines.

zh: 重要的是不要过于专注于常规和日程安排，因为它们会让我们对眼前的事物视而不见。
en: It is important not to become too preoccupied with routines and schedules, because they can make us blind to what is in front of us.

zh: 有时我们需要灵活变通，适应不断变化的环境。
en: Sometimes we need to be flexible and adapt to changing circumstances.

zh: Rob Gueterbock 正在介绍蒙台梭利教育，这种教育鼓励孩子们通过动手和按照自己的节奏追随自己的兴趣来学习。
en: Rob Gueterbock is introducing Montessori education, which encourages children to learn by doing and following their own interests at their own pace.

zh: 五百年前，周一早上6点，当地的文法学校开始上课。
en: Five hundred years ago, at 6 am, on a Monday morning, lessons would begin at the local grammar school.

zh: 课程的主要目标是学习拉丁语语法——当时的国际语言（至少在欧洲是这样）以及所有大学课程的语言。
en: The main aim of the lesson was to study Latin grammar — the international language of the time (in Europe, at least) and the language of all university courses.

zh: 学生都是男孩；如果女孩接受任何教育，她们只能在家里接受。
en: The pupils were all boys; if girls received any education at all, they would get it at home.

zh: 男孩们会拿出他们的石板来学习语法规则和翻译句子。
en: The boys would take out their slates to learn grammatical rules and translate sentences.

zh: 在19世纪中叶欧洲生产出价格实惠的纸张之前，大多数学生使用石板。
en: Before the production of affordable paper in the mid-19th century in Europe, most pupils used slates.

zh: 它们是薄而坚硬的扁平岩石片，用作书写媒介。
en: They were thin pieces of hard flat rock used as a medium for writing.

zh: 男孩们必须一直说拉丁语。
en: The boys would have to speak Latin at all times.

zh: 如果他们完全失败，他们的惩罚是高达50下的藤条鞭打！
en: If they failed at all, their punishment was up to 50 strokes of the cane!

zh: 没有人知道今天有多少人在学习英语——一个常见的估计是十亿人。
en: No one knows how many people are learning English today — one common estimate is one billion people.

zh: 技术在人们学习方式中变得越来越重要：互联网、视频和平板电脑让我们在课堂上更多地接触英语，因为许多教室都有互联网接入、视频设施和交互式白板。
en: Technology has become more and more important in how people learn: the Internet, videos and tablets have exposed us more to English in the classroom, as many classrooms have Internet access, video facilities and interactive whiteboards.

zh: 但在大多数情况下，有一件事没有改变......仍然有老师！
en: But in most cases, one thing hasn't changed ... there's still a teacher!

zh: 谁知道2050年的课堂上会使用什么语言。
en: Who knows what language will be used in the classroom of 2050.

zh: 许多人认为教科书可能很快就会成为过去，大多数学生将使用平板电脑或智能手机代替笔和纸。
en: Many people think that textbooks may soon be a thing of the past and that most students will use tablets or smartphones instead of pen and paper.

zh: 有些人认为英语可能不会像现在这样流行。
en: Some people think that English may not be as popular as it is now.

zh: 中文，甚至是计算机设计的全新国际语言，会取代英语吗？
en: Will Chinese or even a completely new international language designed by computers replace English?

zh: 有一件事是肯定的：技术将成为未来语言教育的核心。
en: There is one thing that is for sure: technology will be at the centre of tomorrow's language education.

zh: 一个有趣的想法是，可能不再需要翻译人员，因为尽管还不完美，但我们已经有了可以做到这一点的软件。
en: One interesting thought is that there may no longer be a need for translators as, although not perfect yet, we already have software to do it.

zh: 玛丽亚·蒙台梭利（1870-1952）是意大利著名的教育家，其教学方法影响了全世界的人们。
en: Maria Montessori (1870-1952) is a famous Italian educationalist whose method of teaching has influenced people all over the world.

zh: 蒙台梭利1870年出生于意大利安科纳省，1896年从医学院毕业后，成为该国第一位女医生。
en: Born in the province of Ancona, Italy, in 1870, Montessori became the first female doctor in her country after she graduated from medical school in 1896.

zh: 后来，在与贫困儿童一起工作时，她在罗马建立了一个“儿童之家”（Casa dei Bambini）。
en: Later, working with deprived children, she set up a "Children's House" (Casa dei Bambini) in Rome.

zh: 这就是她发展蒙台梭利方法的地方，这是一种鼓励非正式教学风格的教育体系。
en: This was the place where she developed the Montessori Method, an educational system that encourages an informal style of teaching.

zh: 孩子们通过处理日常材料来学习，并按照自己的节奏发展。
en: Children learn from handling everyday materials, and they develop at their own pace.

zh: 蒙台梭利的哲学很简单。
en: The Montessori philosophy is simple.

zh: 例如，其原则之一是，孩子是独特的个体，必须能够自由学习而不受批评或限制。
en: For instance, one of its principles is that children are unique individuals who must be free to learn without being criticised or restricted.

zh: 是孩子控制着节奏、主题和课程，而不是班上的其他同学或老师。
en: It is the child that controls the pace, topic and lessons, not the rest of the class or the teacher.

zh: 因此，孩子们享受学习，这给了他们信心并让他们感到快乐。
en: As a result, children enjoy learning, and this gives them confidence and makes them happy.

zh: 蒙台梭利方法还教授孩子们技能，以帮助他们变得独立。
en: The Montessori Method also teaches children skills to help them become independent.

zh: 很小的孩子就学会自己穿衣、做饭以及收拾玩具和衣服。
en: Very young children learn to dress themselves, to cook and to put their toys and clothes away.

zh: 鼓励孩子们随心所欲地重复活动，并通过进行不同的活动来发展他们的观察技能。
en: Children are encouraged to repeat activities as often as they wish, and they develop their observation skills by doing different activities.

zh: 蒙台梭利教师密切观察孩子，以便为他们提供个性化的学习计划。
en: A Montessori teacher observes children closely in order to provide them with individual learning programmes.

zh: 教师是引导者，而不是课堂的领导者，帮助孩子们睁开眼睛看到周围的奇迹。
en: The teacher is a guide, not a leader of the classroom, helping to open children's eyes to the wonders around them.

zh: 玛丽亚·蒙台梭利想要解放孩子们的思想，以便他们能够通过自我教导和自我修正来学习。
en: Maria Montessori wanted to free children's minds so that they could learn by self-teaching and self-correction.

zh: 这是一种鼓励孩子们通过动手和实验来学习的教学方法。
en: It is an approach to teaching which encourages children to learn through doing and experimenting.

zh: 蒙台梭利学校的一个典型房间里有许多孩子们可以使用的东西，例如书籍、物品和游戏。
en: A typical room in a Montessori school has many things children can use, for example, books, objects and games.

zh: 家具很轻，因此他们可以随意摆放；橱柜很低，因此孩子们可以够到。
en: The furniture is light so they can arrange it as they wish, and the cabinets are low so the children can reach them.

zh: 因为环境提供了一系列活动，孩子们喜欢一起工作，并且他们发展出一种基于合作而非竞争的社会生活。
en: Because the environment offers a range of activities, children like to work together, and they develop a social life based on cooperation rather than competition.

zh: 但如今许多人在学校实践蒙台梭利理念时并没有完全理解它，因此其效果受到质疑。
en: But many people nowadays do not understand fully the belief of Montessori when they practise it at school so the effects are questioned.

zh: 并且由于该方法鼓励非正式教学，正式教学和教师的作用在儿童的社会化过程中可能被忽视。
en: And because the method encourages informal teaching, the formal teaching and the role of teacher may be neglected in children's socialisation.

zh: 玛丽亚·蒙台梭利周游世界，例如，遍及欧洲、美国，甚至印度，她培训教师如何使用她的方法。
en: Maria Montessori travelled all over the world, for instance, throughout Europe, to the United States, and even to India, and she trained teachers how to use her method.

zh: 正是在她的晚年，她建立了教师培训中心，这些中心将推动她的工作继续前进。
en: It was in her later years when she established the teacher-training centres that would take her work forward.

zh: 现在在亚洲、欧洲和北美有许多学校使用蒙台梭利的课程和方法。
en: There are now many schools in Asia, Europe and North America which use the Montessori curriculum and methods.

zh: 她写了许多关于教育的书籍和文章。
en: She wrote many books and articles about education.

zh: 她的两部重要著作包括写于1912年的《蒙台梭利方法》和写于1936年的《童年的秘密》。
en: Two of her important works include The Montessori Method, written in 1912, and The Secret of Childhood, written in 1936.

zh: 她的著作已被翻译成多种语言，以便世界各地的人们能更多地了解蒙台梭利的哲学。
en: Her works have been translated into many languages so that people around the world can learn more about the Montessori philosophy.

zh: 在她生命的后期，她专注于利用教育来促进世界和平。
en: Later in her life, she focused on using education to promote world peace.

zh: 因其工作，她在多个国家获得了许多奖项，并在三个不同的年份被提名诺贝尔和平奖。
en: For her work she received many awards in several countries and was nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize in three different years.

zh: 她于1952年在荷兰去世，享年82岁。
en: She died in the Netherlands in 1952 at the age of 82.

zh: 海伦·凯勒是一个非凡的女孩。
en: Helen Keller was an exceptional girl.

zh: 当她还是个孩子的时候，她无法交流，因为她既看不见也听不见。
en: When she was a child, she couldn't communicate because she couldn't see or hear.

zh: 由于这些限制，她经常惹麻烦且行为不端。
en: As a result of these restrictions, she was often troublesome and badly behaved.

zh: 幸运的是，一位出色的老师安妮·沙利文被请来帮助海伦。
en: Fortunately, Anne Sullivan, who was a superb teacher, was brought in to help Helen.

zh: 安妮有一个简单的方法来教海伦词汇。
en: Anne had a simple technique for teaching Helen vocabulary.

zh: 例如，安妮会让海伦玩一个娃娃，然后在她的手上拼写“D-O-L-L”。
en: Anne would, for example, let Helen play with a doll, and then spell "D-O-L-L" on her hand.

zh: 通过这种方式，海伦在脑海中建立了这个词的图像。
en: In this way, Helen built a mental picture of the word.

zh: 安妮也把这种方法用于其他单词。
en: Anne used this technique for other words as well.

zh: 例如，她把海伦的手放在一股液体中来教“水”。
en: For example, she put Helen's hand in a stream of liquid to teach "water".

zh: 随着海伦知识的扩展，安妮开始教更复杂的单词。
en: As Helen's knowledge expanded, Anne started to teach more complex words.

zh: 然而，海伦学习像“思考”这样的抽象概念仍然很困难，因为她无法触摸它们，海伦经常感到失望。
en: However, it was still hard for Helen to learn abstract concepts like "think" because she couldn't touch them, and Helen was often disappointed.

zh: 幸运的是，有一天，她有了一个突破。
en: Luckily, one day, she had a breakthrough.

zh: 她在做项链时犯了一些错误。
en: She was making necklaces when she made some mistakes.

zh: 她不知道如何修复它们，所以她停下来思考。
en: She didn't know how to fix them, so she stopped to think.

zh: 当她这样做时，安妮摸了摸海伦的头，并在她手上写下了“思考”。
en: When she did this, Anne touched Helen's head and wrote "think" on her hand.

zh: 这是海伦第一次理解这样一个抽象的单词！
en: It was the first time Helen had understood such an abstract word!

zh: 我认为教育有三个主要目标。
en: I think education has three main objectives.

zh: 第一是获取知识。
en: The first is to gain knowledge.

zh: 某些知识对于追求职业和为社会做贡献是必要的。
en: Certain kinds of knowledge are necessary to pursue a career and to contribute to society.

zh: 例如，我们需要学习数学来理解商业。
en: For example, we need to learn maths to understand business.

zh: 但教育也让我们理解世界是如何运作的。
en: But education also lets us understand how the world works.

zh: 它让我们接触到各种各样的想法，并让我们洞察他人的思维方式。
en: It exposes us to a variety of ideas and gives us insight into how other people think.

zh: 第二，教育超越了知识的获取——它为学习者提供了发展技能、独立思考以及终身学习的工具。
en: Secondly, education goes beyond the acquisition of knowledge — it provides learners with the tools to develop skills, to think for themselves and to learn throughout their lives.

zh: 第三，教育建立核心价值观，并为你的社会生活做准备。
en: Thirdly, education establishes core values and prepares you for social life.

zh: 它帮助你理解对与错，探索真理，为自己的行为负责并与他人互动。
en: It helps you understand what is wrong and right, explore the truth, take responsibility for your actions and interact with others.

zh: 它也有助于你正直地行事，无论你的背景或环境如何。
en: It also helps you behave with integrity, regardless of your background or circumstances.

zh: 总的来说，教育之所以重要，不仅因为它促进知识的获取，还因为它促进个人的智力、道德和社会发展。
en: In general, education is important not only because it facilitates the acquisition of knowledge, but also because it promotes an individual's intellectual, moral and social development.

zh: 受人尊敬的古代哲学家们有他们关于事物如何运作的理论：尽管他们的大部分理论已被证明是错误的，但他们工作的影响至今仍能感受到。
en: Respected ancient philosophers had their theories about how things worked: Even though most of their theories have been proved wrong, the influence of their work is still felt today.

zh: 阅读我们如何获取知识和理解是很有趣的。
en: It is interesting to read how we acquire knowledge and understanding.

zh: 如今，科学家使用科学方法来分析人们如何学习。
en: Today, scientists use scientific method to analyse how people learn.

zh: 他们从做出假设开始，然后尝试去证明它们。
en: They start by making assumptions and then try to prove them.

zh: 传统智慧说我们应该努力学习，这是对的，但几乎每个人都同意，当我们放松和快乐时，我们的表现会更好。
en: Conventional wisdom says that we should study hard, and this is true, but almost everyone agrees that we perform better when we are relaxed and happy.

zh: 萨姆的生活被一位了不起的老师改变了。
en: Sam had his life changed by one amazing teacher.

zh: 他一直是一个努力学习的学生，但由于一些糟糕的考试成绩，他的信心受到了打击。
en: He had always been a hard-working student but he had his confidence knocked due to some bad exam results.

zh: 他的老师埃里克森女士注意到萨姆在课堂上表现不佳，显得疲惫和孤僻。
en: His teacher Ms Eriksson, noticed that Sam was not doing well in class and seemed tired and withdrawn.

zh: 她的担忧也得到了另一位老师的认同，即如果情况不改变，萨姆可能会许多科目不及格。
en: She also had her concerns shared by another teacher that Sam might fail many of his subjects if things didn't change.

zh: 埃里克森女士在早上上课前与萨姆一起工作，分享如何管理时间的建议。
en: Ms Eriksson worked with Sam before classes started in the morning, sharing advice on how to manage his time.

zh: 她还帮助萨姆练习呼吸和放松技巧。
en: She also helped Sam to practise breathing and relaxation techniques.

zh: 有了所获得的学习技巧和建议，萨姆觉得他的信心增加了。
en: With the study tips and advice he had been given, Sam felt that he had his confidence increased.

zh: 当萨姆在年底拿到他的考试成绩时，他简直不敢相信——他几乎所有的科目都得了满分！
en: When Sam got his exam results at the end of the year, he could not believe it — he had gotten almost full marks in all subjects!

zh: 他永远不会忘记他那位出色的老师向他展示的善意和支持。
en: He will never forget the kindness and support his wonderful teacher had shown him.

zh: 作为记者，我们是美的传播者。
en: As journalists, we are the disseminators of beauty.

zh: 我们将生活中最美的事物传播给人民，让他们知道世界上什么是美的，什么是丑的。
en: We disseminate the most beautiful things in life to the people, letting them know what is beautiful and what is ugly about the world.

zh: 电影是三种世界通用语言之一，另外两种是数学和音乐。
en: Film is one of the three universal languages, the other two: mathematics and music.

zh: 我认为，一份好的报纸是一个民族与自己的对话。
en: A good newspaper, I suppose, is a nation talking to itself.

zh: 许多小事通过正确的广告宣传而被放大。
en: Many a small thing has been made large by the right kind of advertising.

zh: 读书和看电影是两种截然不同的体验，但当根据一本喜爱的书改编成电影时，抱有很高的期望是很正常的。
en: Reading a book and watching a film are two very different experiences, but it's normal to have high expectations when a film of a favourite book is made.

zh: 有很多次，我对自己喜爱的一本书改编成的电影感到悲观甚至失望。
en: There are many times I have been pessimistic or even disappointed by a film of a book I love.

zh: 虽然备受推崇的书并不总能拍出好电影，但可以肯定的是，伟大的电影可能是由并非特别好的书改编而成的。
en: Although highly regarded books do not always make good films, it is safe to say that great films may be made from not particularly good books.

zh: 《教父》立刻浮现在脑海中。
en: The Godfather immediately springs to mind.

zh: 电影的原素材可能取自经典小说、短篇故事、漫画书和舞台剧，以及非虚构作品，如传记和自传，甚至是那些由代笔作家写的作品。
en: The source material for a film may be taken from classic novels, short stories, comic books and stage plays, as well as non-fiction such as biographies and autobiographies, even those written by ghost writers.

zh: 所有这些都可以运作得很好，但为什么许多改编作品乃至翻拍作品在影院观众和评论家那里都失败了呢？
en: All can work well, but why do many adaptations and indeed remakes fail with both cinema audiences and critics?

zh: 为什么有些改编作品得到的评分比其他作品低？
en: And why do some adaptations get lower ratings than others?

zh: 一个关键问题显然是电影与原著有多接近。
en: A key question is obviously how close to the original the film is.

zh: 由于一部典型的电影只有大约两个小时长，这就成了编剧们需要决定什么适合搬上银幕以及什么需要省略的问题。
en: Since a typical film is only around two hours long, it becomes a question for screenwriters to decide what is appropriate for the screen and what to leave out.

zh: 编剧们享有创作自由，有时可能会有情节上的改动、增补，甚至不同的结局，以取悦制片人、导演和试映观众。
en: Screenwriters take creative liberty and sometimes there may be changes to the plot, additions, and even different endings to please producers, directors and test audiences.

zh: 我和许多人一样，经常在离开电影院时感觉“电影不像书”。
en: I, like many people, have often left the cinema feeling "the film is not like the book".

zh: 另一个问题领域是演员阵容。
en: Another problem area is the cast.

zh: 找到电影观众能接受的演员可能意味着成功与失败的区别。
en: Finding actors acceptable to film audiences can mean the difference between success and failure.

zh: 这本书的读者运用他们的想象力来想象人物，并对人物应该长什么样、声音如何有非常明确的想法。
en: Readers of the book use their imaginations to visualise characters and have very definite ideas about how the characters should look and sound.

zh: 有些书由于技术原因太难改编，尽管像《少年派的奇幻漂流》这样的电影正在改变这种观念：CGI（计算机生成图像）技术对电影产业产生了巨大影响。
en: Some books are just too difficult to be adapted for technical reasons, although films like The Life of Pi are changing this idea: CGI (Computer Generated Image) technology has had a huge impact on the film industry.

zh: 它使得以前无法拍摄的内容成为现实。
en: It allowed the previously unfilmable to become a reality.

zh: 人们可能认为书的作者是进行电影改编的最佳人选。
en: One may think that it is the book writer who is in the best position to do film adaptations.

zh: 事实上，有时作者会深度参与这个过程，甚至可能担任编剧。
en: In fact, sometimes authors are very involved in the process and may even be the screenwriter.

zh: 这实际上可能是一个缺点，因为他们可能过于贴近原始材料，发现很难适应一种新的形式。
en: This can actually be a disadvantage because they may be too close to the material and find it difficult to adapt to a new form.

zh: 在其他时候，他们并不参与，并且有作家对其作品的电影版本非常不满的例子；斯蒂芬·金就是其中之一。
en: At other times they are not, and there are instances where writers have been very unsatisfied with the film versions of their work; Stephen King is an example of this.

zh: 更为极端的是小说家J·D·塞林格，他确保他广受欢迎的小说《麦田里的守望者》永远不可能被拍成电影。
en: Even more extreme was novelist J. D. Salinger, who made sure no film versions of his popular novel A Catcher in the Rye could ever be made.

zh: 在这种情况下，显然观众不会失望！
en: In this situation, it is clear that viewers will not be disappointed!

zh: 对我来说，无论如何，去看一部你喜爱的书改编的电影总是一个坏主意。
en: For me, anyway, it is always a bad idea to watch a film of a book you love.

zh: 尽管存在挑战，但也有一些由畅销书改编而成的非常成功的电影。
en: Despite the challenges, there have been some highly successful films made from popular books.

zh: 对我来说，《大白鲨》、《哈利·波特》和《指环王》都属于这一类。
en: For me, Jaws, Harry Potter and The Lord of the Rings all fit this category.

zh: 而可能证明改编成功的最好例子就是詹姆斯·邦德系列，其中大部分改编自伊恩·弗莱明的007小说。
en: And possibly the best proof of a successful adaptation is the James Bond series, the majority of which has been adapted from the 007 novels of Ian Fleming.

zh: 也许不同寻常的是，观众似乎接受了扮演詹姆斯·邦德的演员频繁更换，尽管每个人似乎都有自己最喜欢的。
en: Perhaps unusually, audiences seem to accept that the actors who have played James Bond have changed so often, although everyone seems to have their favourite.

zh: 广告业以向可能需要商品的人们推广和销售商品为基本目的而开始。
en: The advertising industry started with the fundamental purpose of promoting and selling goods to people who might need them.

zh: 广告公司会创建一种基本的广告类型，称为商业广告。
en: Advertising agencies would create a basic type of advertisement known as a commercial advertisement.

zh: 这些广告多年来随着社会、经济和技术的发展经历了一个变化的过程。
en: Such advertisements have gone through a process of change over the years along with social, economic and technological developments.

zh: 经典广告过去常常提供关于寻找特定产品的信息。
en: Classic advertisements used to give information about finding certain products.

zh: 它很可能是一个公司的名称，其中一部分可能包含产品原产地的名称。
en: It was likely to be the name of a company, part of which might include the name of the place from which the product originated.

zh: 例如，它可能是“上海缝纫机”或“青岛啤酒公司”。
en: For example, it could be "Shanghai Sewing Machines", or "Tsing Tao Beer Corporation".

zh: 从名称中你会知道公司销售什么，他们在哪里，以及产品的名称。
en: From the name you would know what the company sells, where they are located, and the name of the product.

zh: 广告公司过去常常创建这种广告，并且它可能非常有效。
en: Advertising agencies used to create this kind of advertising and it could be very effective.

zh: 但随后他们意识到，这可能不适合产品和服务相似的市场。
en: But then they realised that it might not be suitable for a marketplace where products and services are similar.

zh: 尤其是在现代，广告需要吸引并保持公众的注意力。
en: Particularly now, in modern times, advertisements need to attract and hold the attention of the public.

zh: 因此，现在看到由专家提供的图片或文字组成的广告来向人们展示产品有多好，是很正常的。
en: For this reason, it's now normal to see advertisements that consist of pictures or words from experts to show people how good the product is.

zh: 另一些则试图创作人们就是喜欢看的广告。
en: Others try to create advertisements that people simply enjoy looking at.

zh: 由于当今的广告商通常拥有大笔预算，大量资金被用于运用现代设计技术，使这些广告在视觉上尽可能吸引人。
en: As present-day advertisers often have large budgets, huge amounts of money are spent on applying modern design techniques to make these advertisements as visually attractive as possible.

zh: 然而，即使这样，在现代城市中也常常不足以引起注意。
en: However, even this is often not enough to be noticed in a modern city.

zh: 由于广告的激增，人们已经习惯了看到高标准的视觉设计。
en: Because of the boost in advertising, people are used to seeing a high standard of visual design.

zh: 现代广告需要别的东西来将自己与竞争对手区分开来。
en: Modern advertisements need something else to separate them from their competitors.

zh: 为了在竞争激烈的广告世界中脱颖而出，它们必须将最高标准的设计与富有创意的方式结合起来，让人们相信他们“必须拥有这个产品”。
en: To stand out in a world of competitive advertising, they must combine the highest standard of design with creative ways to make people believe that they "must have the product".

zh: 例如，一些广告可能会暗示购买汽车能让你自由。
en: For example, some advertisements may suggest that buying a car can make you free.

zh: 也就是说，他们利用自由的概念来销售汽车。
en: That is, they use the concept of freedom to sell the car.

zh: 其他常见的理念包括幸福、财富和成功。
en: Other common ideas include happiness, wealth and success.

zh: 事实上，在广告世界里，这样的内容就和广告本身一样古老。
en: In fact, in the world of advertisements, such content is as old as advertising itself.

zh: 对当今许多广告商来说，重复旧理念并非成功的途径。
en: For many of today's advertisers, repeating old ideas is not a successful approach.

zh: 过去行之有效的理念现在确实不那么有效了。
en: Ideas that would work in the past genuinely are not as effective now.

zh: 他们意识到，与产品相关联的理念无论多么吸引人都无关紧要——大多数人都知道并预料到广告的主要目的是让顾客购买产品。
en: They realise that it does not matter how attractive the idea linked with the product is — most people know and anticipate that the main purpose of the advertisement is to make customers buy the product.

zh: 相反，这些广告商寻找其他方法来让人们注意到他们的产品。
en: Instead, these advertisers look for other ways to make people notice their products.

zh: 当今顶尖的广告商认为，运用幽默以及新颖独特的想法来让人们感到惊喜是很重要的。
en: The top advertisers of today believe that using humour as well as new and unusual ideas to surprise people is important.

zh: 他们的目标是创造出前所未有且人们觉得有趣的东西。
en: Their aim is to create something that has never been seen before and that people find interesting.

zh: 通过这样做，他们希望让人们忘记有人正试图向他们推销东西！
en: By doing this, they hope to make people forget that someone is trying to sell them something!

zh: 然而，并非所有广告都是为了盈利而销售产品和服务。
en: However, not all advertising is about selling products and services for a profit.

zh: 如今一些广告旨在为社会和福利事业做出贡献。
en: Some advertisements nowadays aim to make a contribution to society and welfare.

zh: 例如，有一些公益广告，鼓励公民参与改善社区、保护环境以及帮助他人。
en: For example, there are public advertisements, which encourage citizens to participate in improving their neighbourhood, protecting the environment, and helping other people.

zh: 在过去十年中，政府赞助了广告来教育公众防治艾滋病、节约用水、帮助穷人、戒烟等。
en: Over the last decade, the government has sponsored advertisements to educate the public on fighting AIDS, saving water, helping the poor, giving up smoking, etc.

zh: 这些广告有助于增加公众对那些过去被大多数人忽视的话题的了解，这使得我们的社会对每个人来说都变得更美好。
en: These advertisements have helped to increase public knowledge about topics that used to be ignored by most people, and this has made our society a better place for everyone.

zh: 《美女与野兽》（1946年）由让·科克托执导，改编自博蒙夫人创作的童话故事。
en: Beauty and the Beast (1946), directed by Jean Cocteau, is based on the fairy tale by Madame Le Prince de Beaumont.

zh: 让·马雷饰演野兽，若塞特·戴饰演美女。
en: Jean Marais is the Beast and Josette Day is Beauty.

zh: 美女是一位法国商人的三个女儿之一。
en: Beauty is one of the three daughters of a French trader.

zh: 她的姐妹费利西和阿德莱德把美女当作仆人对待。
en: Her sisters, Felicie and Adelaide, treat Beauty as a servant.

zh: 一天，美女的父亲在森林里迷路了，但发现了一座奇怪的城堡，他走了进去，寻求帮助。
en: One day, Beauty's father gets lost in the forest, but finds a strange castle, which he enters, looking for help.

zh: 城堡的主人是一个半人半兽的异常生物，他告诉商人——要么他的一个女儿代替他成为城堡的囚犯，要么他就会死。
en: The owner of the castle is an abnormal creature, half-man, half-beast, who tells the trader — either one of his daughters replaces him as a prisoner in the castle, or he will die.

zh: 美女主动提出代替她的父亲，被困在城堡里。
en: Beauty offers to replace her father and be trapped in the castle.

zh: 最后，她发现野兽并不像他看起来那样可怕和残忍。
en: In the end, she discovers the Beast is not as awful and cruel as he seems.

zh: 事实上，他是一位英俊的王子。
en: In fact, he is a handsome prince.

zh: 这部电影以一种非常有趣和巧妙的方式处理了表象的主题。
en: The film deals with the theme of appearances in a very interesting and clever manner.

zh: 在一个生动的场景中，美女照镜子，她的脸变成了野兽的脸。
en: In one vivid scene, Beauty looks in the mirror and her face is transformed into the Beast's.

zh: 这部电影还使用了一些巧妙的特效。
en: The film also uses some clever special effects.

zh: 例如，窃窃私语的家具和现代电影中的任何东西一样可怕。
en: For example, the whispering furniture is as frightening as anything in modern films.

zh: 然而，这部电影确实有其缺点。
en: The film does, however, have its faults.

zh: 野兽的声音相当奇怪，结尾处恋人飞翔的场面有点傻。
en: The voice of the Beast is rather strange, and the lovers flying at the end is a bit silly.

zh: 不过，综合考虑，它一定是有史以来最美丽、最辉煌的电影之一。
en: All things considered, though, it must be one of the most beautiful and splendid films ever made.

zh: 尽管它是黑白的，但对光线的惊人运用有时让它看起来像一幅流动的绘画。
en: Although it is in black and white, the striking use of light makes it seem at times like a moving painting.

zh: 《美女与野兽》传达了一个我们都应该清楚的信息——你不应该以貌取人。
en: Beauty and the Beast conveys a message that should be clear to all of us — you shouldn't judge a book by its cover.

zh: 尽管这个版本既没有可爱的角色也没有美妙的歌曲，但它能引起所有年龄段人群的共鸣。
en: Although it has neither cute characters nor wonderful songs in this version, it speaks to people of all ages.

zh: 我推荐给全家人观看。
en: I recommend it for the whole family.

zh: 研究表明，我们醒着的时间有一半是以某种形式接触大众媒体。
en: Research has shown that half of our waking hours are spent in contact with mass media in one form or another.

zh: 报纸和杂志的读者数量正在下降。
en: Newspaper and Magazine readership is declining.

zh: 在美国，发行量最大的杂志是针对50岁以上人群的。
en: The magazine with the highest circulation in the US is for people over 50.

zh: 在英国，是一本电视节目列表杂志。
en: In the UK, it's a TV listings magazine.

zh: 在德国，是一本汽车杂志。
en: In Germany, it's a car magazine.

zh: 在加拿大，是一本烹饪杂志。
en: In Canada, it's a cooking magazine.

zh: 在英国，收视率最高的电视节目是肥皂剧的一集。
en: The most watched TV show in the UK is an episode of a soap opera.

zh: 在美国，是电视剧的一集。
en: In the US, it is an episode of a drama series.

zh: 在加拿大、印度和新西兰，是体育赛事的报道。
en: In Canada, India and New Zealand, it is coverage of a sports event.

zh: 许多国家的青少年每天有多达四个小时的屏幕时间。
en: Young teenagers in many countries get up to four hours of screen time a day.

zh: 其中大部分仍然是电视。
en: Most of that is still TV.

zh: 所有在线时间中有25%花在社交媒体上。
en: 25% of all online time is spent on social media.

zh: 它是最具互动性的媒体形式。
en: It is the most interactive form of media.

zh: 每秒有一百万分钟的视频被上传和下载。
en: One million minutes of video is uploaded and downloaded every second.

zh: 收到每天都能见到的自己母亲的信一定有点奇怪，但我希望你能读一读，并认真对待我要说的话。
en: It must seem a little odd to get a letter from your own mother whom you see every day, but I hope you will read this and take what I have to say seriously.

zh: 我在你12岁生日时给你买了一部手机，因为你非常想要，而且你所有的朋友都有。
en: I bought you a phone for your 12th birthday because you wanted one so much, and all your friends have one.

zh: 我知道你爱你的手机，但我不知道你是否意识到你使用它的频率有多高。
en: I know you love your phone, but I wonder whether you are aware of just how much you use it.

zh: 从我的角度来看，你似乎一直在查看手机上的通知或消息。
en: From my perspective, it seems that you are constantly checking your phone for notifications or messages.

zh: 你甚至在我们全家在餐厅吃饭或我在和你说话时也这样做。
en: You even do this when we're having a family meal in a restaurant or when I'm talking to you.

zh: 你可能没有意识到这一点，但每当你感到哪怕一点点无聊，你就会查看手机。
en: You may not realise this, but whenever you feel even slightly bored, you check your phone.

zh: 你似乎从未放松过。
en: You never seem to relax.

zh: 你总是处于警觉状态，我认为这对你不太好。
en: You're constantly on alert, and I don't think this is very good for you.

zh: 我们都需要时不时地关掉它。
en: We all need to switch it off every now and then.

zh: 如果我要求我们外出时你把手机留在家里，你就会生闷气、变得焦躁不安，几乎就像你在遭受戒断症状一样。
en: And if I ask you to leave your phone at home when we go out, you sulk and get twitchy, almost as if you're suffering withdrawal symptoms.

zh: 就好像你的手机是一种毒品。
en: It's like your phone is a drug.

zh: 我曾试图和你谈这件事，但每当我要求你放下手机看着我时，你都说“等一下”。
en: I have tried to talk to you about this, but whenever I ask you to put down your phone and look at me, you say "in a minute".

zh: 不幸的是，“等一下”从未到来，我们也从未交谈。
en: Unfortunately, "in a minute" never comes, and we never talk.

zh: 我实际上记不起我们上一次进行适当的面对面谈话是什么时候了。
en: I can't actually remember the last time we had a proper face-to-face conversation.

zh: 我甚至不得不发信息告诉你晚饭已经上桌了！
en: I even have to message you to let you know dinner is on the table!

zh: 这就是为什么我决定把我的担忧写在这封信里给你。
en: That's why I decided to put my concerns in a letter to you.

zh: 请不要认为我写这封信是为了惩罚你或唠叨你。
en: Please don't think I am writing this letter to punish you or to nag you.

zh: 我知道你是个好孩子。
en: I know you are a good boy.

zh: 我不认为你是想故意伤害我或无礼。
en: I don't think you want to hurt me or be rude intentionally.

zh: 然而，我最近听说了一种叫做“无手机恐惧症”的东西——即害怕没有手机。
en: However, I recently heard about something called "nomophobia" — the fear of being without a phone.

zh: 这是一种真实的疾病，我担心你是否患有它。
en: It's a real illness and I'm worried whether you have it.

zh: 我真的认为，如果我们一起去见一位成瘾专家，一起讨论这个严重的问题，会是一个好主意。
en: I really think it would be a good idea if we all went to see an addiction expert together so we can all have a discussion about this serious problem.

zh: 我知道你不想去，但我非常担心你。
en: I know you won't want to, but I'm very concerned about you.

zh: 请花些时间思考一下我写的内容。
en: Please take some time to think about what I have written.

zh: 收到你的信很奇怪，但我想你觉得这是传达信息的最好方式，因为我总是在玩手机！
en: It was weird getting a letter from you, but I suppose you felt this was the best way of getting your message across because I'm always on my phone!

zh: 老实说，我真的没有想过你可能会有什么感受。
en: To be honest, I haven't really thought about how you might be feeling.

zh: 我没有意识到我让你这么难过。
en: I didn't realise I was upsetting you so much.

zh: 我想我没有想到存在沟通问题，因为我花了很多时间和你在一起。
en: I suppose I didn't think there was a communication problem because I spend so much time with you.

zh: 我花很多时间在手机上，因为我喜欢和我的朋友保持经常联系。
en: I spend a lot of time on my phone because I like to keep in constant contact with my friends.

zh: 我们有很多共同点，也有很多话题可聊。
en: We have so much in common and so much to talk about.

zh: 当我想和他们聊天时，发个信息是如此的容易。
en: And it's so easy to just send a message when I want to chat with them.

zh: 我并没有真正想过控制我花在手机上的时间的重要性。
en: I hadn't really thought about the importance of controlling how much time I spend on my phone.

zh: 这是我需要考虑的事情，所以谢谢你的提醒。
en: This is something I need to think about, so thank you for the reminder.

zh: 我同意我将来需要更加注意我使用手机的程度。
en: I agree that I need to pay more attention to how much I use my phone in the future.

zh: 此外，我会尝试少用手机。
en: Also, I will try to use my phone less.

zh: 如果我同意在晚餐时不看手机，你能否同意我不必去看成瘾专家？
en: If I agree not to look at it during dinner, will you agree I don't have to go to the addiction specialist?

zh: 如果我们能做到这一点，也许我可以尝试更多地戒掉手机。
en: If we manage this, perhaps I can try to wean myself off my phone a bit more.

zh: 我们都有自己喜爱的书，甚至可能是多年来一直吸引着我们心灵和思想的系列书籍。
en: We all have books that we love, perhaps even series of books that have captured our hearts and minds over many years.

zh: 当我们听说它们可能会被改编成电影时，一些人感到兴奋，而另一些人则不高兴。
en: When we hear that they might be adapted into films, some people feel excited while others are unhappy.

zh: 人们常常对他们最喜欢的角色和故事被搬上银幕的可能性感到悲观。
en: Often people are pessimistic about the chances that their favourite characters and stories will be shown on screen.

zh: 当读者如此清晰地想象出它的世界时，问题就出现了。
en: The problem arises when the readers visualise its world so clearly.

zh: 在他们心中，只有某些演员是合适的，而且场景必须像他们想象的那样完美。
en: In their mind there are only certain actors that are appropriate and the setting must be as perfect as they imagined.

zh: 近年来有如此多的书籍被改编，尤其是那些有超级英雄和大量动作场面的书籍。
en: There have been so many adaptations of books in recent years especially books with superheroes and lots of action.

zh: 这些电影的演员阵容出现在多部电影中，并且这些电影已被证明在国际上是成功的。
en: The cast of these films appear in several films and the films have proved to be internationally successful.

zh: 尽管许多评论家喜欢这些电影并给予了很高的评价，但他们也发现这些电影彼此之间感觉非常相似。
en: Although many critics have enjoyed these films and given them high ratings, they have also found that they can feel very similar to each other.

zh: 当然，改编作品来自许多不同类别的文学，比如著名人物的传记。
en: Of course, adaptations come from many different categories of literature, such as biographies of famous figures.

zh: 一些小说家愉快地参与了电影制作，而另一些人，如美国作家J.D.塞林格，则努力确保他们的书永远不会被拍成电影。
en: Some novelists have happily been involved in the film production while others like the American author J. D. Salinger, fought to ensure that their books would never become films.

zh: 互联网的广泛使用对媒体产生了影响。
en: The widespread use of the Internet has made impact on the media.

zh: 虽然传统上，大型公司一直是制作和报道新闻的主体。
en: While traditionally, large corporations have been the ones to produce and report on the news.

zh: 例如，人们可以上传他们认为别人会感兴趣的事件视频。
en: For example, people can upload videos of events they believe are of interest to others.

zh: 电视台，尤其是报纸，受到了负面影响，因为选择付费通过它们做广告的公司变少了。
en: TV stations and especially newspapers have been negatively affected, as fewer companies choose to pay to advertise through them.

zh: 新闻变得具有互动性的另一种方式是通过评论、分享和讨论故事的普及。
en: Another way that news has become interactive is through the popularity of commenting on stories, sharing and discussing them.

zh: 我们正生活在媒体历史上一个不同寻常的时代。
en: We are living in an unusual time in the history of the media.

zh: 过去，人们购买报纸或看电视；现在他们只需在手机上打开一个应用程序或使用电脑即可立即获取新闻。
en: In the past, people bought newspapers or watched TV; they now simply open an app on their mobile phone or use their computer to access the news immediately.

zh: 而手机也意味着现在每个人都可以成为记者。
en: And mobile phones also mean that everyone can now be a reporter.

zh: 人们参与诸如微博之类的活动很常见，他们可以在其中分享他们选择的任何主题的想法和观点。
en: It's common for people to engage in activities such as microblogging where they can share their ideas and opinions on whatever topic they choose.

zh: 虽然这对读者有很多好处，尽管如此，他们正在适应这个新世界，许多报纸现在拥有网站、数字副本和其他与读者联系的方式。
en: While there has been lots of advantages for readers, Despite this, they are adapting to this new world and many newspapers now have websites, digital copies and other ways to connect to readers.

zh: 广告自人们开始销售商品以来就一直存在，但随着技术的发展，这些年来它经历了许多变化。
en: Advertising has existed as long as people have been selling goods, but it has gone through many changes over the years as technological development has occurred.

zh: 技术的发展也通过使用CGI和在互联网上分享广告来帮助广告现代化。
en: Technological development has also helped to modernise advertisements through the use of CGI and sharing advertisements on the Internet.

zh: 然而，广告的基本目的始终是一样的，即鼓励人们购买他们可能需要（但通常不需要！）的商品和服务。
en: The fundamental purpose of advertising has always been the same, however, which is to encourage people to buy goods and services that they may need (but often don't!).

zh: 广告公司与公司合作设计能够吸引公众的广告，他们竭尽全力去了解人们想要什么。
en: Advertising agencies work with companies to design advertisements that will attract the public and they go to great lengths to understand what people want.

zh: 传统上，广告仅包含产品的基本信息、制造地点和所有者。
en: Advertisements traditionally consisted of only basic information about a product, the location where it was made and who owned it.

zh: 最终，很明显这种方法适用于公司直接相互竞争的情况。
en: Eventually it became clear that this approach was suitable for situations where companies were directly competing with each other.

zh: 近年来，广告商的方法发生了变化，现在商业广告的预算和电影一样大是很常见的！
en: In recent years, the approach of the advertisers has changed and now it's common for commercial advertisements to have budgets as big as films!

zh: 现在，支付电影和体育界的名人参与广告活动以进一步鼓励公众购买产品也很常见。
en: It is also common now to pay celebrities from films and sports to participate in advertising campaigns to further encourage the public to buy a product.

zh: 现在，当谈到广告时，目的通常是试图在观看者心中创造一种情感，要么通过使其幽默，甚至悲伤。
en: Now when it comes to advertising, often the aim is to try and create an emotion in the person viewing it, either by making it humorous or even sad.

zh: 广告商还试图将产品与幸福、自由或权力等概念结合起来，告诉公众这种产品将带来这种感觉或结果。
en: Advertisers also try to combine the product with a concept like happiness, freedom or power, telling the public that this product will lead to this feeling or outcome.

zh: 对广告有一些批评和负面看法，因为人们觉得公司不再诚实和直率。
en: There have been some criticisms and negative views on advertising as people feel that companies are no longer being honest and straightforward.

zh: 无论如何，看起来广告在未来几年将继续在我们的社会中扮演非常重要的角色。
en: Regardless, it looks like advertising will continue to play a very important role in our society for years to come.

zh: 我曾经很喜欢看报纸。
en: I used to love reading newspapers.

zh: 每天早餐时我都会从头到尾地读报纸。
en: Every morning at breakfast I'd read the paper from cover to cover.

zh: 如果你这么喜欢看报纸，为什么现在不再看了呢？
en: Why don't you do that anymore if you enjoyed it so much?

zh: 因为现在大家都在网上看报纸。
en: Because everyone reads newspapers online nowadays.

zh: 那为什么这意味着你不能看报纸了呢？
en: So why does that mean that you can't read the newspaper?

zh: 我不想落后于时代。
en: I don't want to be behind the times.

zh: 这对你来说有道理吗？
en: Does that make sense to you?

zh: 我不能说有道理。
en: I can't say it does.

zh: 你能解释得更清楚些吗？
en: Could you explain yourself more clearly?

zh: 我需要让所有人都看到我跟上了现代科技的步伐。
en: I need everyone to see that I am up to date with modern technology.

zh: 好的。我明白你的意思了。
en: OK. I understand what you mean.

zh: 信在中午前刚到，是快递邮件，一个廉价商店的信封，寄信人地址是F·S·莱西，加州美洲豹角。
en: The letter came just before noon, special delivery, a dime-store envelope with the return address F. S. Lacey, Puma Point, California.

zh: 里面是一张一百美元的支票，开给现金，签名是弗雷德里克·S·莱西，还有一张打了很多修改痕迹的普通白色铜版纸。
en: Inside was a cheque for a hundred dollars, made out to cash and signed Frederick S. Lacey, and a sheet of plain white bond paper typed with a number of strikeovers.

zh: 上面写着：约翰·埃文斯先生，亲爱的先生，我从莱恩·埃斯特瓦尔德那里得知您的名字。
en: It said: Mr John Evans, Dear Sir, I have your name from Len Esterwald.

zh: 我的事情紧急且极度机密。
en: My business is urgent and extremely confidential.

zh: 如果可能的话，请于周四下午或晚上来美洲豹角，在印第安首脑酒店登记，并拨打2306找我。
en: Please come to Puma Point, Thursday afternoon or evening, if at all possible, register at the Indian Head Hotel, and call me at 2306.

zh: 一周都没什么生意，但这让今天成了个好日子。
en: There hadn't been any business in a week, but this made it a nice day.

zh: 支票的开户银行大约六个街区远。
en: The bank on which the cheque was drawn was about six blocks away.

zh: 我过去兑了现，吃了午饭，取出车就出发了。
en: I went over and cashed it, ate lunch, and got the car out and started off.

zh: 山谷里很热，圣贝纳迪诺更热，在通往美洲豹湖的高速路上行驶了十五英里，到了五千英尺高，依然很热。
en: It was hot in the valley, hotter still in San Bernadino, and it was still hot at five thousand feet, fifteen miles up the high-gear road to Puma Lake.

zh: 我在五十英里蜿蜒曲折的高速公路上开了四十英里后，才开始凉快起来，但直到我到达水坝，开始沿着湖南岸行驶，经过堆积的花岗岩巨石和远处平地上散乱的营地，才真正凉快下来。
en: I had done forty of the fifty miles of curving twisting highway before it started to cool off, but it didn't really get cool until I reached the dam and started along the south shore of the lake past the piled-up granite boulders and the sprawled camps in the flats beyond.

zh: 我到美洲豹角时已是傍晚，饿得像条被掏空内脏的鱼。
en: It was early evening when I reached Puma Point and I was as empty as a gutted fish.

zh: 印第安首脑酒店是拐角处的一栋棕色建筑，对面是一个舞厅。
en: The Indian Head Hotel was a brown building on a corner, opposite a dance hall.

zh: 我登记后，把手提箱搬上楼，扔进一个阴冷、看起来硬邦邦的房间，地板上有一块椭圆形地毯，角落有一张双人床，光秃秃的松木墙上除了一张五金店日历外什么也没有，日历因干燥的山间夏日而卷曲了。
en: I registered, carried my suitcase upstairs and dropped it in a bleak, hard-looking room with an oval rug on the floor, a double bed in the corner, and nothing on the bare pine wall but a hardware-store calendar all curled up from the dry mountain summer.

zh: 我洗了脸和手，下楼去吃饭......
en: I washed my face and hands and went downstairs to eat ...

zh: 我狼吞虎咽地吃完了他们所谓的常规晚餐，喝了一杯白兰地助消化，然后出去了......
en: I gobbled down what they called the regular dinner, drank a brandy to sit on it, and went out...

zh: 电话局是一个小木屋，角落里有一个带投币电话的亭子。
en: The phone office was a log cabin, and there was a booth in the corner with a coin-in-the-slot telephone.

zh: 我把自己关在里面，投下镍币，拨了2306。
en: I shut myself inside and dropped my nickel and dialled 2306.

zh: 我说：“弗雷德·莱西先生在吗？”
en: I said: "Is Mr Fred Lacey there?"

zh: “莱西先生现在不在这里，埃文斯先生。
en: "Mr Lacey is not here right now, Mr Evans.

zh: 这让她问了我两个问题，而我只问了一个。
en: That gave her two questions to my one.

zh: 我说：“您是莱西太太吗？”
en: I said: "Are you Mrs Lacey?"

zh: “是的。我是莱西太太。”
en: "Yes. I am Mrs Lacey."

zh: 我觉得她的声音听起来紧张过度，但有些声音一直就是那样。
en: I thought her voice sounded taut and over-strung, but some voices are like that all the time.

zh: “是公事，”我说。
en: "It's a business matter," I said.

zh: “他什么时候回来？”
en: "When will he be back?"

zh: 我想是今晚某个时候。
en: Sometime this evening, I suppose.

zh: “您的小屋在哪里，莱西太太？”
en: "Where is your cabin, Mrs Lacey?"

zh: 它在巴尔塞奇角，村庄以西约两英里处。
en: "It's on Ball Sage Point, about two miles west of the village.

zh: 你是从村里打来的吗？
en: Are you calling from the village?

zh: “我一小时后再打来，莱西太太，”我说着挂了电话。
en: "I'll call back in an hour, Mrs Lacey," I said, and hung up.

zh: 我走出电话亭。
en: I stepped out of the booth.

zh: 在房间的另一个角落，一个穿着休闲裤的黑皮肤女孩在一张小桌子上写着某种账本。
en: In the other corner of the room, a dark girl in slacks was writing in some kind of account book at a little desk.

zh: 她抬起头，微笑着说道：“你觉得山里怎么样？”
en: She looked up, smiled and said: "How do you like the mountains?"

zh: “这里非常安静，”她说。
en: "It's very quiet up here," she said.

zh: 你认识一个叫弗雷德·莱西的人吗？”
en: Do you know anybody named Fred Lacey?"

zh: 哦，是的，他们刚装了电话。
en: Oh, yes, they just had a phone put in.

zh: 他们买下了鲍德温的小屋。
en: They bought the Baldwin cabin.

zh: 它空置了两年，他们刚买下它。
en: It was vacant for two years, and they just bought it.

zh: 它在巴尔塞奇角的尽头，一个建在高地上的大木屋，可以俯瞰湖泊。
en: It's out at the end of Ball Sage Point, a big cabin on high ground, looking out over the lake.

zh: 景色非常棒。
