en: It has a marvellous view.

zh: 你认识莱西先生吗？”
en: Do you know Mr Lacey?"

zh: “不认识，”我说着离开了那里。
en: "No," I said, and went out of there.

zh: 我走回印第安酋长旅馆，上了我的车......
en: I walked back to the Indian Head and got into my car...

zh: 我把车停在岬角的尖端，走向一棵倒下的大树，树根翘起在空中十二英尺高。
en: I stopped the car on the tip of the point and walked over to a huge tree fallen with its roots twelve feet in the air.

zh: 我背靠着它坐在干透的地上，点了一斗烟。
en: I sat down against it on the bone-dry ground and lit a pipe.

zh: 这里宁静、安详，远离一切喧嚣。
en: It was peaceful and quiet and far from everything.

zh: 湖的远岸有几艘快艇在玩捉迷藏，但我这边只有寂静的水面，在山间暮色中缓缓变暗。
en: On the far side of the lake a couple of speedboats played tag, but on my side there was nothing but silent water, very slowly getting dark in the mountain dusk.

zh: 我纳闷弗雷德·莱西到底是谁，他想要什么，为什么如果他的事情那么紧急，他既不想待在家里也不留个口信......
en: I wondered who the hell Fred Lacey was and what he wanted and why he didn't want to stay home or leave a message if his business was so urgent ...

zh: 半小时后，我站起来，用脚跟在地上挖了个洞，磕出烟斗里的灰，然后把土踩实盖在灰烬上。
en: At the end of half an hour I got up and dug a hole in the soft ground with my heel and knocked my pipe out and stamped down the dirt over the ashes.

zh: 毫无缘由地，我朝湖的方向走了几步，这让我来到了那棵树的尽头。
en: For no reason at all, I walked a few steps toward the lake, and that brought me to the end of the tree.

zh: 于是我看到了那只脚......
en: So I saw the foot...

zh: 那男人中年，半秃，皮肤晒得很好，留着从嘴唇上方剃上去的一字胡。
en: The man was middle-aged, half bald, had a good coat of tan and a line moustache shaved up from the lip.

zh: 他的嘴唇很厚，嘴巴微微张开，像通常那样，露出大而坚固的牙齿。
en: His lips were thick, and his mouth, a little open as they usually are, showed big strong teeth.

zh: 他有一张那种衣食无忧、没什么烦恼的脸。
en: He had the kind of face that goes with plenty of food and not too much worry.

zh: 他的眼睛望着天空。
en: His eyes were looking at the sky.

zh: 我似乎无法与他对视。
en: I couldn't seem to meet them.

zh: 绿色运动衫的左侧被血浸透了，血迹有一个餐盘那么大。
en: The left side of the green sport shirt was sodden with blood in a patch as big as a dinner plate.

zh: 在那片血迹的中间，可能有一个烧焦的洞。
en: In the middle of the patch there might have been a scorched hole.

zh: 光线变得有点难以捉摸......
en: The light was getting a little tricky ...

zh: 他的钱包里有十二美元和一些卡片，但让我感兴趣的是他影印驾照上的名字。
en: There was twelve dollars in his wallet and some cards, but what interested me was the name on his photostat driver's licence.

zh: 我划了根火柴，以确保在渐暗的光线下我没看错。
en: I lit a match to make sure I read it right in the fading light.

zh: 驾照上的名字是弗雷德里克·希尔德·莱西。
en: The name on the licence was Frederick Shield Lacey.

zh: 我去年突然生病了。
en: I became ill last year very suddenly.

zh: 我总是咳嗽、打喷嚏，还失去了食欲。
en: I always coughed and sneezed and lost my appetite.

zh: 我的视力变差了，牙齿有时还会出血。
en: My vision became bad and my teeth bled sometimes.

zh: 我非常害怕，决定去看医生。
en: I was very afraid and decided to see the doctor.

zh: 当我在医院候诊室等待与医生会诊时，一位老妇人主动与我交谈。
en: While I was at the hospital waiting for a consultation with the doctor, an old lady engaged me in conversation in the waiting room.

zh: 她问我怎么了，当我告诉她时，她天真地从口袋里拿出一个苹果说：“我能请你吃这个美味的橙子吗？”
en: She asked me what was wrong and when I told her, she innocently took an apple from her pocket and said, "Can I interest you in this delicious orange?"

zh: 起初我很困惑，告诉她她拿的是一个苹果。
en: At first I was confused and told her she was holding an apple.

zh: 然后她说：“嗯，在我看来你的眼睛似乎没问题。
en: Then she said, "Well, your eyes seem fine to me.

zh: 现在如果我能让你吃了这个，那你就可以回家了！”
en: Now if I can just get you to eat this then you can go home!"

zh: 这真的让我笑了起来，让我振作了起来！
en: This really made me laugh and cheered me up!

zh: 我意识到她看出我感到沮丧和担忧，她在试图帮助我。
en: I realised that she saw that I was feeling depressed and worried and she was trying to help me.

zh: 医生给我检查后，告诉我情况不严重，我不需要做任何手术。
en: After the doctor examined me, he told me that it wasn't serious and that I wouldn't need any surgery.

zh: 现在我仍然记得那位女士和那个“橙子”。
en: Now I still remember that lady and the "orange".

zh: 我很感激她在我危机时刻让我笑了。
en: I'm thankful that she made me laugh in a time of crisis.

zh: 许多研究表明幽默和笑对心理有益。
en: There have been many studies which show the psychological benefits of humour and laughing.

zh: 积极的情绪让我们感觉更有活力，并与更好的健康和幸福感相关。
en: A positive mood makes us feel far more energised and is associated with better health and well-being.

zh: 人们笑的原因有很多；有时他们因为一个笑话或电影、电视节目中有趣的情节而笑。
en: People laugh for lots of reasons; sometimes they laugh at a joke or funny plot of films or TV shows.

zh: 人们甚至在感到尴尬、羞愧或经历焦虑时也会笑，或者偶尔甚至以牺牲他人为代价（这不是一件好事）。
en: People laugh even when they are feeling embarrassed or ashamed or experiencing anxiety or occasionally even at the cost of others (which is not a nice thing to do).

zh: 令人惊讶的是，即使是微笑的面部动作也能让你感到快乐，即使你并不快乐！
en: Surprisingly, even the facial movement of smiling can make you feel happy even if you are not!

zh: 你可以假装笑，你的身体会以为你真的快乐并放松下来！
en: You can pretend a laugh and your body will think you really are happy and relax!

zh: 所以，记住要尽可能多地笑和微笑，以增强你的幸福感并改善你的生活。
en: So, remember to laugh and smile as much as possible in order to enhance your happiness and improve your life.

zh: 我昨晚第一次去看了一场喜剧表演。
en: I went to a comedy show for the first time last night.

zh: 真是太有趣了！
en: It was so much fun!

zh: 晚上有几位喜剧演员表演，他们都有自己独特的风格。
en: There were several comedians performing during the night, who had their unique style.

zh: 有一位来自澳大利亚的年轻表演者，她以一种轻松的方式谈论她的国籍和文化，真的让我很开心。
en: There was a young performer from Australia who really amused me as she spoke about her nationality and culture in a light-hearted way.

zh: 我非常喜欢它。
en: I loved it a lot.

zh: 尽管她来自另一个国家，但有些有趣的方面是普遍的，因为我们在生活中都会遇到它们。
en: Even though she was from another country, there were funny aspects that are just universal as we all encounter them in life.

zh: 另一位出色的表演者是一位英国人，他一边弹钢琴一边唱有趣的歌曲。
en: Another excellent performer was an Englishman who sang funny songs while playing the piano.

zh: 我们都对他的才华感到惊讶。
en: We were all astonished by his talent.

zh: 我以前听说过他的名字，因为他有很好的声誉。
en: I had heard his name before as he has quite a good reputation.

zh: 他的幽默非常现代，而且他主要谈论时事。
en: His humour was very contemporary and he mostly spoke about current events.

zh: 我很少像昨晚那样笑得那么多，我迫不及待想再去一次！
en: I rarely laugh as much as I did last night and I can't wait to go again!

zh: 朱利叶斯·亨利·马克思（1890-1977），更广为人知的名字是格劳乔·马克思，是一位美国喜剧演员、电影明星和电视节目主持人。
en: Julius Henry Marx (1890-1977), better known as 'Groucho Marx, was an American comedian, film star and TV host.

zh: 他以机智敏捷著称，被广泛认为是一位喜剧天才。
en: He was famous for his quick wit and is widely considered a comic genius.

zh: 格劳乔以其浓密的眉毛、画上去的胡子、眼镜、尖细的嗓音、弯腰走路的姿势和雪茄而闻名。
en: Groucho was well-known for his bushy eyebrows, painted-on moustache, glasses, high-pitched voice, stooped walk and cigar.

zh: 他是马克思兄弟组合的领袖。
en: He was the leader of the group the Marx Brothers.

zh: 马克思兄弟是一个美国家庭喜剧表演团体，从1905年到1949年，在歌唱、百老汇和电影领域都取得了成功。
en: The Marx Brothers was an American family comedy act that was successful in singing, on Broadway, and in films from 1905 to 1949.

zh: 该组合通常以他们的艺名而闻名：奇科、哈波、格劳乔、格莫和泽波。
en: The group were usually known by their stage names: Chico, Harpo, Groucho, Gummo, and Zeppo.

zh: 马克思兄弟于194年解散，此后格劳乔开始独自表演。
en: The Marx Brothers broke up in 1949, after which Groucho started performing on his own.

zh: 格劳乔拍摄了26部电影，其中13部是与他的兄弟奇科和哈波合作的。
en: Groucho made 26 films, 13 of them with his brothers Chico and Harpo.

zh: 有趣的是，在马克思兄弟的电影中，格劳乔几乎总是扮演那些有着不寻常的名字、中间名首字母和不太可能的姓氏的角色。
en: Funnily enough in the Marx Brothers films, Groucho almost always played characters with unusual first names, a middle initial and unlikely last names.

zh: 其中两个是：奥蒂斯·B·德里夫特伍德，鲁弗斯·T·菲莱弗莱。
en: Two of these were: Otis B. Driftwood, Rufus T. Firefly.

zh: 他们一些比较著名的电影包括《鸭羹》、《歌剧院之夜》和《动物饼干》等。
en: Some of their better known films are Duck Soup, A Night at the Opera, and Animal Crackers among others.

zh: 格劳乔在1929年华尔街股灾和大萧条中在股票交易所亏了钱。
en: Groucho lost money on the stock exchange in the Wall Street Crash of 1929 and Great Depression.

zh: 他结过三次婚，并与所有三任妻子都离婚了。
en: He was married three times and divorced all three of his wives.

zh: 他是一档广播和热门电视智力竞赛节目《你赌你的人生》的主持人，该节目播出于1947年至1960年。
en: He was the host of a radio and popular television quiz show You Bet Your Life which spanned the years 1947-1960.

zh: 关于他的一个有趣的事实是，他从未在舞台上说过脏话，这与一些现代喜剧演员相去甚远。
en: An interesting fact about him was that he never swore on stage, which is a far cry from some of the modern comedians.

zh: 关于他的另一个有趣的事实是，他在摇滚明星埃尔维斯·普雷斯利去世三天后去世。
en: Another interesting fact about him was that he died three days after the rock and roll star, Elvis Presley.

zh: 这意味着他的去世没有获得本应获得的关注。
en: This meant his death didn't receive the attention it would normally have received.

zh: 在狗之外，书是人类最好的朋友。
en: Outside of a dog, a book is man's best friend.

zh: 在狗里面，太黑了没法看书。
en: Inside of a dog it's too dark to read.

zh: 那些是我的原则，如果你不喜欢它们......嗯，我还有别的。
en: Those are my principles, and if you don't like them... well, I have others.

zh: 一天早上，我穿着我的睡衣射杀了一头大象。
en: One morning I shot an elephant in my pyjamas.

zh: 它是怎么钻进我的睡衣里的，我永远也不会知道。
en: How he got into my pyjamas I'll never know.

zh: 我是一所特殊教育学校的老师。
en: I am a teacher of a special education school.

zh: 一些学生有身体残疾，例如视力或听力问题。
en: Some students have physical disabilities like problems with their eyesight or hearing.

zh: 其他人有学习困难，这意味着他们在阅读和社交方面有严重的障碍。
en: Others have learning difficulties, which means that they have severe disorders in reading and socialisation.

zh: 起初，他们的行为有时很麻烦，但现实是这些孩子无法控制自己的行为。
en: Initially, their behaviours are sometimes troublesome but the reality is that these children cannot control what they are doing.

zh: 不要过于温和地管教他们，因为这不是他们的错，这一点很重要。
en: It's important not to discipline them too gently as it is not their fault.

zh: 相反，我会温和地解释我希望他们做什么，并帮助他们。
en: Instead, I gently explain what I would like them to do and help them.

zh: 我的一些学生词汇量很大，拼写也很好，而另一些则很吃力。
en: Some of my students have large vocabulary and can spell words well, while others struggle.

zh: 每次我的学生取得突破，例如，他们学会一项新技能或克服一个挑战，我都感到非常高兴。
en: Every time my students have a breakthrough, for example, they learn a new skill or overcome a challenge, I feel so happy.

zh: 但当然，如果一个学生不能取得进步，我从不失望——一切都会在它自己的时间里发生。
en: But of course, if a student cannot make progress I'm never disappointed — everything happens in its own time.

zh: 所有的孩子都是宝贵的，值得被爱、关心和教育。
en: All children are precious and deserve love, care and education.

zh: 在我那个年代，教育的目标是读、写和数学，除此之外别无其他。
en: Back in my days, the objective of education was reading, writing and mathematics and after that nothing else mattered.

zh: 学校并不被期望为年轻人发展成为全面发展的个体做出贡献。
en: Schools were not expected to contribute to the development of young people as well-rounded individuals.

zh: 提供道德教育并教导孩子拥有良好品格被视为家庭的责任。
en: It was seen as the responsibility of the family to provide moral education and teach their children to have good character.

zh: 此外，人们相信，仅仅通过遵守法律，人们就会成为好公民。
en: Besides, it was believed that just by following the law, people would become good citizens.

zh: 如今，对教育的定义是从更广泛的意义上理解的。
en: Nowadays, the definition of education is understood in much broader terms.

zh: 现在很清楚，教育的真正优先事项是培养全面发展的人，而不仅仅是提供学习的信息。
en: It is clear now that the true priority of education is to develop the whole person rather than just provide information to learn.

zh: 当然，读、写和数学仍然是核心科目，但还有其他重要的技能和能力需要掌握，例如自主学习和主动性、判断力和诚信、创造力和批判性思维。
en: Certainly, reading, writing and maths are still core subjects but there are other important skills and abilities to acquire, such as autonomous learning and initiative, judgement and integrity, creativity and critical thinking.

zh: 同样，课堂外的活动也有助于发展孩子的个性和品格。
en: Likewise, activities outside of the classroom can help develop children's personalities and characters.

zh: 我们现在也知道，父母和其他家庭成员可以激发孩子对学习的兴趣，并扮演重要的角色。
en: We now also know that parents and other family members can stimulate their children's interest in learning and have an important role to play.

zh: 我们生活在现代世界真是太幸运了。
en: We're so lucky to live in the modern world.

zh: 我们现在对科学和自然了解得更多了。
en: We know so much more about science and nature now.

zh: 过去人们的信仰相当基础。
en: People had rather basic beliefs in the past.

zh: 几百年前，关于科学和世界有很多误解，例如，那个被认为是真理的想法——地球是平的。
en: There were a lot of misunderstandings about science and the world hundreds of years ago, the idea which was thought to be true that the Earth was flat, for example.

zh: 值得庆幸的是，对恒星和行星的研究现在已经发展了很多。
en: Thankfully the study of the stars and planets has developed so much now.

zh: 我们欠很多像伽利略这样的人一份情，因为他们站出来反对那个拒绝相信地球不是行星和恒星中心的宗教组织。
en: We are in debt to a lot of people like Galileo for standing up against the religious organisation which refused to believe that the Earth wasn't the centre of the planets and stars.

zh: 不过我们应该记住，未来的人们可能也会认为我们知道的很少。
en: We should remember though that in the future people might also think we knew very little.

zh: 也许我们对一些基本科学原理的研究和检验是不正确的。
en: Perhaps our studies and examinations of some basic scientific principles are incorrect.

zh: 这么想真是太奇怪了！
en: That's so strange to think!

zh: 当然，这就是为什么我们始终保持学习和质疑是绝对重要的。
en: Of course that is why it is absolutely important that we always keep learning and questioning.

zh: 是的，我总是建议并试图说服人们运用他们的批判性思维技能并保持开放的心态。
en: Yes, I always advise and try to persuade people to use their critical thinking skills and keep an open mind.

zh: 大家好！感谢查看我的博客！
en: Hi all! Thanks for checking out my blog!

zh: 我上周学到了一些非常有趣的东西，我想与你们分享！
en: I learnt something really interesting last week that I thought I'd share with you!

zh: 你知道有四种不同的学习风格吗？
en: Did you know there are four different learning styles?

zh: 视觉型学习者通过看东西学习效果最好。
en: Visual learners learn best by seeing things.

zh: 他们在学习时需要看图片和图像。
en: They need to see pictures and images when learning.

zh: 他们还需要语言包含大量的心理图像。
en: They also need language to include lots of mental images.

zh: 视觉型学习者会喜欢听老师说“想象你是......”作为一项活动。
en: A visual learner would enjoy hearing a teacher say "Imagine you are..." as an activity.

zh: 我认为视觉型学习者是非常有效的学习者。
en: I think that a visual learner is a very effective learner.

zh: 听觉型学习者通过听来学习效果最好。
en: Auditory learners learn best by listening.

zh: 这些学习者通常非常仔细地听，并深入思考他们所听到的内容。
en: These learners usually listen very carefully and think deeply about what they hear.

zh: 他们更喜欢学习活动中的一切都被解释给他们听，而不是必须阅读说明。
en: They prefer learning activities where everything is explained to them rather than having to read instructions.

zh: 他们非常有逻辑性，并且会花时间提供答案。
en: They are very logical and take their time to provide answers.

zh: 动觉型学习者喜欢做事并使用他们所有的感官。
en: Kinesthetic learners enjoy doing things and using all of their senses.

zh: 为了成功学习，他们需要“现实生活”的例子，并喜欢进行实验。
en: In order to learn successfully they need "real-life" examples and enjoy conducting experiments.

zh: 他们不喜欢坐着，只是听或看东西。
en: They do not enjoy sitting and simply listening or looking at things.

zh: 在我看来，动觉型学习者玩得最开心！
en: To me it seems that a kinesthetic learner has the most fun!

zh: 这里有一个新的给你。
en: And here's a new one for you.

zh: “读写型”学习者喜欢传统的课堂学习方法，例如列清单、记笔记以及用自己的话写东西。
en: "Read-Write" learners enjoy traditional classroom approaches to learning such as list-making, note-taking and writing things in their own words.

zh: 他们也喜欢多项选择题。
en: They also enjoy multiple choice questions.

zh: 事实证明我是一个视觉型学习者，因为我热爱艺术和戏剧，在这些领域我们必须将情境可视化。
en: It turns out that I am a visual learner as I love art and also drama where we have to visualise situations.

zh: 了解你偏好的风格非常有帮助，因为它可以帮助你设计策略，这些策略将有助于你在现在和未来的学习中取得成功。
en: It's really helpful to understand your preferred style as it can help you design strategies that will help you be successful when learning both now and in the future.

zh: 麦吉尔大学正在为其新的学位课程“广告与新媒体”招生。
en: McGill University is now enrolling for its new degree course "Advertising and New Media".

zh: 该课程旨在让毕业生对广告和营销的基本原理有扎实的理解。
en: The aim of this programme is to give graduates a solid understanding of the basic principles of advertising and marketing.

zh: 教学方法由独立学习、基于项目的团队合作以及与讲师进行的课堂授课组成。
en: The teaching approach is made up of independent study, project-based teamwork and in-class tuition with lecturers.

zh: 鼓励学生参与社区相关项目，通过信息宣传活动支持当地社区。
en: Students are encouraged to engage in community related projects to support local neighbourhoods with information campaigns.

zh: 拥有这个学位，毕业生将获得职业晋升，并对广告公司极具吸引力。
en: With this degree, graduates will get a career step up and be highly desirable to advertising companies.

zh: 由于他们将学习到与技术相关的技能，毕业生也将非常适合其他行业，如互联网和社交媒体公司。
en: Graduates would also be a good fit for other industries such as Internet and social media companies, thanks to the technology-related skills they will learn.

zh: 我们预计这门课程将吸引大量兴趣，所以如果您对广告和新媒体真正感兴趣，请尽快申请。
en: We expect that this course will draw to it a lot of interest so please apply soon if you are truly interested in advertising and new media.

zh: 《天堂之路》是著名且广受欢迎的作家兼导演迪·奥康纳的一部新电影。
en: The Road to Heaven is a new film by the famous and well-liked writer and director, Dee O'Connor.

zh: 这个故事是关于一群朋友决定一起环游世界。
en: The story is about a group of friends who decide to travel around the world together.

zh: 他们期望事情会顺利进行，但由于一位不速之客，事情肯定没有如愿。
en: They expect things will go smoothly but thanks to an unexpected guest, it most certainly doesn't.

zh: 喜剧不是我偏爱的类型，所以我对看这部电影并不兴奋；然而，我必须说我非常喜欢它！
en: Comedy is not my preferred genre so I wasn't excited about watching this; however, I must say I loved it!

zh: 我发现他们启用了一批新的不知名演员很不寻常，这很冒险，但在这里却取得了成功。
en: I found it unusual that they used a cast of new and unknown actors, which is risky, but here it pays off.

zh: 片长两小时，对于这种类型的电影来说长度正好。
en: At two hours long, it is just the right length for this type of film.

zh: 总的来说，它轻松、有趣且令人兴奋——是您全家的完美选择。
en: Overall, it is light, fun and exciting — a perfect choice for your whole family.

zh: 如果您已经看过并且有任何意见要分享，请在此下方评论并告诉我们您的想法。
en: If you have seen it already and have any opinions to share, please comment under here and let us know what you think.

zh: 顾客：在有大量观众的时候做广告更好吗？
en: Customer: Is it better to advertise when there are lots of viewers?

zh: 观众的数量很重要，但他们也必须是合适的观众类型。
en: The number of viewers is important but they also have to be the right kind of viewers.

zh: 顾客：我不太明白。
en: Customer: I don't quite understand.

zh: 代理人：当然，观众需要是那些实际上对你的产品感兴趣的人，也就是说，人口统计数据必须正确。
en: Agent: Sure, the viewers need to be those who would actually be interested in buying your product, that is, the demographics have to be right.

zh: 顾客：是的，我想知道的是这如何影响广告的价格。
en: Customer: Yes, what I want to know is how this affects the price of the advert.

zh: 代理人：我们需要找到一个你的潜在客户正在看电视的时间段，这个收视率将决定价格。
en: Agent: We need to find a time slot in which your potential customers are watching TV, and this viewership will determine the price.

zh: 这对你来说清楚了吗？
en: Is that clear to you?

zh: 广告无处不在，它似乎已经成为我们生活中不可避免的一部分。
en: Advertising is all around us and it seems to have become an unavoidable part of our life.

zh: 数字营销专家估计，大多数美国人每天可能会接触到大约4000到10000条广告。
en: Digital marketing experts estimate that most Americans may be exposed to around 4000 to 10 000 advertisements a day.

zh: 你可能认为你永远不会被广告欺骗并被说服购买东西，但在你决定之前先读读这篇文章！
en: You may think you will never fall for an ad and be persuaded to buy something, but read the article first before you decide!

zh: 虽然直接的硬销售广告可能很容易被发现——比如那些由名人代言的产品——但软销售广告可能更难被发现。
en: While direct hard-sell ads might be easy to detect — such as those for products endorsed by celebrities — soft-sell ads may be more difficult to spot.

zh: 广告商使用的一种方法是让顾客相信有立即购买产品的需要，使用诸如“赶快”、“立即行动”、“最后机会”、“机不可失”等词语或短语。
en: One such method advertisers use is to make customers believe there is an immediate need to buy the product using words or phrases such as hurry, act now, last chance, now or never.

zh: 当然，这些并不是唯一的方法。
en: These are not the only methods, of course.

zh: 其他方法包括赠送样品和赞助活动。
en: Other methods include giving away samples and sponsorship of events.

zh: 事实上，有许多方法适用于不同地方的不同产品。
en: In fact, there are many methods which work for different products in different places.

zh: 伦敦的一家广告公司证实了这一点，称他们的广告是根据具体情况量身定做的。
en: advertising agency in London, confirms this, stating their adverts are tailor-made according to the situation.

zh: 并非所有广告都是针对最终客户的。
en: Not all ads are directed towards the end customer.

zh: 广告公司制作广告时会考虑到投资者和店主。
en: Advertising agencies will make adverts with the investors and shop owners in mind.

zh: 一个令人印象深刻且昂贵的广告可能会向投资者表明该广告公司是成功的，从而可能鼓励他们对其进行投资。
en: An impressive and expensive ad may signal to the investors that the ad agency is successful and they may then be encouraged to invest in it.

zh: 同样，店主更有可能储备广告做得好的产品，因为他们知道顾客更有可能购买这些产品。
en: Similarly, shop owners are more likely to stock well-advertised products because they know customers are more likely to buy them.

zh: 通过这种方式，即使广告不是针对顾客的，它们也能帮助销售产品。
en: In this way, ads can help sell products even when they are not directed at the customer.

zh: 广告在商业的各个阶段都起作用，也许，这就是广告的成功之处。
en: Ads work in all stages of a business and, perhaps, that is the success of advertising.

zh: 现在完成进行时表示动作从过去某一时间开始，一直持续到现在，或者始于过去，现在刚刚终止，但注重的是结果。
en: We use the present perfect continuous tenses for actions that started in the past and continue in the present, or actions that started in the past and have just finished but we are interested in the result.

zh: 现在完成进行时的构成为: have / has been + 动词的 ing 形式。
en: We form the present perfect continuous tense with have / has been + main verb-ing.

zh: 我已经等了他一个小时了。
en: I have been waiting for him for the past hour.

zh: 他们过去三周一直在做他们的项目。
en: They have been working on their project for the past three weeks.

zh: 崇安整个星期都在和他哥哥打架。
en: Chongan has been fighting with his older brother all week.

zh: 艾学习了一整天。
en: Ai has been studying all day.

zh: 她最近参加了很多赛跑。
en: She has been running in a lot of races recently.

zh: 我一整天都在找我的智能手机。
en: I have been looking for my smartphone all day.

zh: 她最近发现这项工作很有挑战性。
en: She's been finding the work challenging lately.

zh: 我写这本书已经写了一整年，希望能很快完成。
en: I have been writing the book all year and am hoping to finish it soon.

zh: 你一整天都在找你的书吗？
en: Have you been looking for your book all day?

zh: 过去完成进行时表示过去某时开始并持续到过去某时的动作。
en: We use the past perfect continuous tense to describe an ongoing action that started in the past and continued for some time in the past.

zh: 过去完成进行时的构成为: had been + 动词 ing 形式。
en: We form the past perfect continuous tenses with had been + main verb-ing.

zh: 在我找到她之前，我已经走了一上午。
en: I had been walking around all morning before I found her.

zh: 我们花了将近一个小时试图找到正确的路，最后才发现了它。
en: We had been trying to find the right road for nearly an hour before we finally spotted it.

zh: 他们看这个系列剧已经看了三个月了。
en: They had been watching the series for three months.

zh: 她一直在寻求帮助，但没有人理会。
en: She had been asking for help but no one took any notice.

zh: 因为下了一整天的雨，一切都是潮湿的。
en: Everything was damp as it had been raining all day.

zh: 她以优异的成绩通过了考试，因为她一整年都在非常努力地学习。
en: She had passed the exam with flying colours because she had been studying very hard throughout the year.

zh: 当他终于到达时，我们已经等了他一个多小时。
en: We had been waiting for him for over an hour when he finally arrived.

zh: 将来进行时表示将来某时持续进行的动作。
en: We use the future continuous tense to talk about an action that will be in progress (an ongoing or continued action) at some time in the future.

zh: 将来进行时的构成为：will be + 动词ing形式。
en: We form the future continuous tense with will be + main verb-ing.

zh: 十一点不要来，因为我要照顾我的侄女。
en: Don't come at eleven because I will be babysitting my niece.

zh: 下个月的这个时候，我们将躺在沙滩上晒太阳。
en: By this time next month, we will be lying on the beach sunbathing.

zh: 你明天会用你的课本吗？
en: Will you be using your textbook tomorrow?

zh: 我们不会待到很晚，因为我明天早上有个早会。
en: We won't be staying late as I have an early meeting tomorrow morning.

zh: 如果我现在不完成这个，明年我还会在读它！
en: If I don't finish this now, I'll still be reading it next year!

zh: 你的叔叔将在假期来看望我们。
en: Your uncle will be visiting us for the holidays.

zh: 明年我们将学习关于伽利略·伽利莱的知识。
en: We will be learning about Galileo Galilei next year.

zh: 副词用来修饰动词、形容词或其他副词。
en: Adverbs modify or tell us more about a verb, an adjective or another adverb.

zh: 副词通常表示方式、地点、时间、频率和程度。
en: Adverbs usually answer the questions of how, where, when, how often and to what extent.

zh: 方式副词表示一件事情是如何做的：
en: Adverbs of manner tell us how something is done:

zh: 小女孩玩得很开心。
en: The little girl played happily.

zh: 地点副词表示事情发生的地点:
en: Adverbs of place tell us where something takes place:

zh: 派对将在这里举行。
en: The party will take place here.

zh: 时间副词表示事情发生的时间:
en: Adverbs of time tell us when something takes place:

zh: 我们昨天看了那部电影。
en: We saw the film yesterday.

zh: 频率副词表示事情发生的频率:
en: Adverbs of frequency tell us how often something takes place:

zh: 我们经常探望家人。
en: We visit our family often.

zh: 我们很少去镇上。
en: We rarely go to town.

zh: 程度副词表示事情发展的程度:
en: Adverbs of degree tell us to what extent something is done:

zh: 他们对结果非常满意。
en: They were very pleased with the results.

zh: 曾春对这个结果感到非常高兴。
en: Zeng Chun was absolutely delighted with the outcome.

zh: have / get something done 结构用来表达要别人为自己做某事。
en: We use the structure have / get something done when we want to say that we ask someone else to do something for us.

zh: 我修好了洗衣机。
en: I fixed the washing machine.

zh: 我请人修好了我的洗衣机。
en: I had my washing machine fixed.

zh: 这本书的电影非常精彩。
en: The film of the book was amazing.

zh: 它在每一个细节上都忠实于原著故事。
en: It stuck to the original story in every last detail.

zh: 这部电视剧甚至更好。
en: The TV series was even better.

zh: 它成为最受欢迎的电视节目之一，并赢得了许多奖项。
en: It became one of the most popular TV shows and won many awards.

zh: 这部电视剧非常出色。
en: The TV series was excellent.

zh: 很明显，所有的书......
en: It is clear that all books ...

zh: 学习一门外语很重要。
en: It is important to learn a foreign language.

zh: 买书而不读是没有用的。
en: It is no use buying books without reading them.

zh: 我母亲的视力在下降，这让我很担心。
en: It worried me that my mother's eyesight was declining.

zh: 它也可以被用作形式宾语。
en: it can also be used as a formal object.

zh: 然后“it”指代后面的从句，特别是用在enjoy, hate, like, love等动词之后的从句。
en: Then "it" refers to a clause that comes after, particularly after verbs such as enjoy, hate, like, love, etc.

zh: 我们总是喜欢你和我们待在一起。
en: We always like it when you stay with us.

zh: 我讨厌人们满嘴食物说话。
en: I hate it when people talk with their mouths full of food.

zh: 当从句、动词不定式或动词-ing作有宾语补足语的宾语时，it可以被用作形式宾语，通常用在think, feel, find, take, make等动词之后。
en: It can be used as a formal object when the clause or verb + infinitive or verb-ing that acts as the object and has an object complement, particularly after verbs such as think, feel, find, take, make, etc.

zh: 我认为人们学习一门外语很重要。
en: I think it important that people should learn a foreign language.

zh: 我发现学习一门外语很重要。
en: I found it important to learn a foreign language.

zh: 我认为买书而不读是没有用的。
en: I think it no use buying books without reading them.

zh: 它可以用作虚主语。
en: It can be used as a dummy subject.

zh: 每个句子都需要主语。
en: Every sentence needs a subject.

zh: 如果某个句子没有主语，可以用上it。
en: If it doesn't have a subject, we use it.

zh: it可以用来指代时间、日期和天气等。
en: We use it to talk about things such as time, dates and weather.

zh: 已经十二点了。
en: It's twelve o' clock already.

zh: 这里很冷。
en: It's very cold in here.

zh: 我们用它来指代自己或其他人：
en: We use it to refer to ourselves or someone else:

zh: 是我。让我进来！
en: It's me. Let me in!

zh: 它可以引导分裂句。
en: It can be used as a pronoun that leads a cleft sentence.

zh: 分裂句是一种复杂的句子（有主句和从句）。
en: A cleft sentence is a complex sentence (it has a main and a dependent clause).

zh: 分裂句通常把从句放在主句前面来强调从句。
en: A cleft sentence usually has the dependent clause in front of the main clause to give it greater emphasis.

zh: 直到我看了电影，我才意识到我有多爱那本书。
en: It wasn't until I saw the film that I realised how much I had loved the book.

zh: 分裂句把新信息和已知信息联系起来。
en: A cleft sentence connects new information to what is already known to the listener.

zh: 我们要离开的时间是十二月（新信息——十二月；已知信息——我们要离开）
en: It is December that we are going away (new information — December; already known information — that we are going away)

zh: 我们使用 used to 和 would 来谈论过去经常发生但现在不再发生的事情。
en: We use used to and would to talk about things that happened regularly in the past but no longer happen now.

zh: 她过去拥有的朋友比我认识的任何人都多。
en: She used to have more friends than anyone else I knew.

zh: 他过去常常一条裤腿比另一条短地走来走去。
en: He would walk around with one trouser leg shorter than the other.

zh: 我们使用 would 来描述动作，使用 used to 来描述动作和状态。
en: We use would to describe actions and used to to describe actions and states.

zh: 我们过去常常在秋天去采蘑菇。
en: We would go mushroom-picking in autumn.

zh: 我的小妹妹过去非常害羞。
en: My little sister used to be very shy.

zh: 他不知所措，不知道是继续打我还是帮助赵先生，所以他只是呆呆地站着。
en: He was confused and didn't know if he should carry on hitting me, or stop and help Mr Zhao — so he just stood there.

zh: 欢笑是最好的良药。
en: Laughter is the best medicine.

zh: 一位著名的艺术收藏家正穿过城市，这时他注意到一只脏猫在商店门口的一个碟子里舔牛奶。
en: A famous art collector was walking through the city when he noticed a dirty cat lapping milk from a saucer in the doorway of a store.

zh: 他惊讶地又看了一眼。
en: He did a double take.

zh: 其意思是它将对我们如何生活产生巨大影响。
en: It means that it will have a great influence on how we live.

zh: 我们都知道如果我们心情不好，我们可能会表达负面的态度和感情。
en: We all know that if we are in a bad mood, we are likely to express negative attitudes and feelings.

zh: 然而，当服务员没有注意的时候，他却忙着把生肉藏在他能够到的任何地方——糖碗、小花瓶、小圆面包里和盘子下面。
en: When the waiters are not looking, however, he busies himself hiding the raw meat anywhere he can reach — the sugar bowl, the tiny flower vase, inside a bread roll and under a plate.

zh: 他的父亲非常严厉，不相信电视的价值。
en: His father was very strict and did not believe in the value of television.

zh: 他朝那个年轻的柜员大吼，以便让在他后面排队的乘客都听到：“你知道我是谁吗？”
en: He screamed at the young agent so that the passengers waiting in line behind him could hear, "DO YOU KNOW WHO I AM?"

zh: 比如，近现代第一位伟大的教育哲学家夸美纽斯认为，在教学时，内容应该适合学生的兴趣，教学方法应该与他们的心智发展相一致。
en: Well, for example, the first great educational philosopher of modern times, Comenius, insisted that when teaching, the subject matter should be adapted to learners' interests, and the method of instruction should be consistent with their mental development.

zh: 我认为，教育不仅仅是传授未来生活所必需的知识和技能，而是在学习使用知识和技能解决现实世界问题的同时，提供一种丰富的人生经历。
en: Well, I think it means that education is not simply about passing on knowledge and skills necessary for a future life, but to provide a rich experience of living while learning to use that knowledge and those skills to solve real-world problems.

zh: 要培养德智体美劳全面发展的社会主义建设者和接班人。
en: We should build an education system which comprehensively fosters the students' all-round moral, intellectual, physical and aesthetic grounding with a hard-working spirit.

zh: 由于海伦理解了语言的关键，她非常渴望学习更多语言，并且尽可能运用它。
en: Now that Helen grasped the key to language, she was very eager to learn more and use it as much as she could.

zh: 她正在安妮的帮助下做项链，这时她发现自己犯了一些错。
en: She was making necklaces with the help of Anne when she noticed that she had made some mistakes.

zh: 瞬间我就明白了，我头脑中进行的活动就是这个词的意思。
en: In a flash I knew that the word was the name of the process that was going on in my head.

zh: 它使我们深入了解人们是如何思考的，以及为了理解不同文化背景的人我们需要采取什么态度。
en: It gives us insights into how people think and the attitude we need in order to understand people of different cultures.

zh: 它帮助你理解对错，探索真理和发展认知，无论事情是否容易或他人的意见如何。
en: It helps you to understand what is wrong and what is right, explore the truth and develop cognition, regardless of whether it's easy or what others' opinions are.

zh: 首先，我们需要问题。
en: To start with, we need questions.

zh: 起初，人们赞同他的研究并敦促他继续，但后来当他
en: At first, people approved of his studies and urged him to continue, but later when he

zh: 证实亚里士多德有错时，他们生气了，并把他送进了监狱。
en: proved Aristotle wrong, they grew angry and put him in prison.

zh: 是给花浇水的时候了。
en: It's time to water the flowers.

zh: 不过于拘泥于常规日程安排是很重要的，因为它们会让我们忽视眼前的事物。
en: It is important not to become too preoccupied with routines and schedules, because they can make us blind to what is in front of us.

zh: 有时我们需要灵活和适应变化的环境。
en: Sometimes we need to be flexible and adapt to changing circumstances.

zh: 我们新闻记者是美的传播者。
en: As journalists, we are the disseminators of beauty.

zh: 要把生活中最美的东西传播给人民，让大家知道世界上什么是美的，什么是丑的。
en: We disseminate the most beautiful things in life to the people, letting them know what is beautiful and what is ugly about the world.

zh: 电脑生成动画技术对电影产业有重大影响。
en: CGI (Computer Generated Image) technology has had a huge impact on the film industry.

zh: 可能最好的例子是詹姆斯·邦德系列，该系列的大部分作品都改编自伊恩·弗来明的《007》小说。
en: And possibly the best proof of a successful adaptation is the James Bond series, the majority of which has been adapted from the 007 novels of Ian Fleming.

zh: 结果，大型媒体公司正在一个个倒闭。
en: As a result, big media corporations are being cut out.

zh: 与社会、经济和技术发展过程同步，多年来这些广告经历了一个变化的过程。
en: Such advertisements have gone through a process of change over the years along with social, economic and technological developments.

zh: 现代广告需要另外一些东西，以便将它们与其竞争者区别开来。
en: Modern advertisements need something else to separate them from their competitors.

zh: 为了在竞争激烈的广告界凸显出来，他们必须将最高水准的设计与创造性的方法结合起来，使人们确信他们“必须购买这种产品”。
en: To stand out in a world of competitive advertising, they must combine the highest standard of design with creative ways to make people believe that they "must have the product".

zh: 他们意识到与产品关联的理念有多么吸引人并不重要—大多数人都明白，广告的主要目的就是让顾客购买产品。
en: They realise that it does not matter how attractive the idea linked with the product is — most people know and anticipate that the main purpose of the advertisement is to make customers buy the product.

zh: 比如公益广告，它鼓励市民参与改善社区、保护环境和帮助他人的活动。
en: For example, there are public advertisements, which encourage citizens to participate in improving their neighbourhood, protecting the environment, and helping other people.

zh: 马华，什么让你笑？
en: What makes you laugh, Ma Hua?

zh: 有很多事情。
en: There are so many things.

zh: 我喜欢有趣的故事和相声，我也喜欢喜剧。
en: I love funny stories and cross talks, and I also like comedies.

zh: 我最喜欢的喜剧类型是当有人假装成别人。
en: My favourite kind of comedy is when someone pretends to be someone else.

zh: 是的，我也喜欢那样。
en: Yes, I like that, too.

zh: 但是为什么这些事情会让我们笑呢？
en: But why do these things make us laugh?

zh: 嗯，我认为是因为幽默的情节和有趣的行为。
en: Well, I think it is because of the humorous plots and funny acts.

zh: 当人们模仿别人时，我们会笑他们能模仿的口音、他们模仿面部表情的方式以及模仿人的举止。
en: When people imitate someone else, we laugh at the accents they are able to imitate, the way they imitate facial expressions and the manner of the people.

zh: 你知道为什么笑对我们有好处吗？
en: Do you know why it is good for us to laugh?

zh: 研究表明，笑有显著的好处；它能缓解压力和焦虑，放松全身，并给人带来幸福感。
en: Research shows that laughing has significant benefits; it relieves stress and anxiety, relaxes the whole body and gives one a sense of well-being.

zh: 你知道，笑还能促使大脑产生天然的感觉良好的化学物质。
en: You know, laughing also encourages the brain to produce natural feel-good chemicals.

zh: 所以，它有助于加强人际关系和促进团队合作。
en: So, it helps to make relationships stronger and enhance teamwork.

zh: 所有这些关于笑的谈话让我想看一些有趣的东西。
en: All this talk of laughter has made me want to watch something funny.

zh: 我们去找一部喜剧看吧！
en: Let's go and find a comedy to watch!

zh: 贾兰，你永远不会相信我发生了什么事——我想我永远也忘不了它。
en: You'll never believe what happened to me, Jia Lan — I don't think I'll ever live it down.

zh: 你知道我一直在学拳击。
en: You know I've been learning to box.

zh: 前几天我进行了我的第一场拳击比赛，赵先生是裁判——你知道他是一个多么严格的老师。
en: I had my first boxing match the other day and Mr Zhao was the referee — and you know what a strict teacher he is.

zh: 我们都怕他。
en: We're all terrified of him.

zh: 在第二回合，我被对手重重一击，感到头晕和不稳。
en: In the second round, I took such a hard hit from my opponent that I felt dizzy and unbalanced.

zh: 我不确定我在哪里。
en: I wasn't sure where I was.

zh: 在防守中，我以一个狂野的挥拳反击。
en: In defence, I attacked back with a wild swing.

zh: 它是如此狂野，以至于没有击中我的对手，而是击中了赵先生的鼻子。
en: It was so wild that instead of the hit landing on my opponent, it landed on Mr Zhao's nose.

zh: 结果，他倒下了！
en: As a result, he collapsed!

zh: 我跑到他身边，试图扶他起来。
en: I ran up to him and tried to help him up.

zh: 我一遍又一遍地说对不起。
en: I said sorry over and over again.

zh: 他推开我，振作起来，站了起来。
en: He pushed me away, gathered himself and got up to his feet.

zh: 你一定感觉很难受。
en: You must have felt so bad.

zh: 我太尴尬了，希望地板能裂开把我吞没。
en: I was so embarrassed that I wanted the floor to open up and consume me.

zh: 他很困惑，不知道是应该继续打我，还是停下来帮助赵先生——所以他只是站在那里。
en: He was confused and didn't know if he should carry on hitting me, or stop and help Mr Zhao — so he just stood there.

zh: 赵先生——然后发生了什么？
en: And Mr Zhao — what happened then?

zh: 他把空着的那只手放在我的肩膀上，说：“孩子，你很有潜力。
en: He put his free hand on my shoulder and said, "Son, you've got real potential.

zh: 那是一次出色的击球！”
en: That was an excellent hit!"

zh: 早上好，感谢大家今天来听我的演讲。
en: Good morning and thank you for joining me today.

zh: 我今天演讲的题目是“我们为什么需要幽默？”
en: The title of my speech today is "Why do we need humour?"

zh: 我相信我们所有人都喜欢看喜剧电影或听一个好笑话，因为它总能让我们开怀大笑。
en: I believe that all of us enjoy watching a comedy film or hearing a good joke, because it makes us laugh all the time.

zh: 婴儿在会说话之前很久就会笑了。
en: Babies laugh long before they can speak.

zh: 这几乎就像是幽默感是与生俱来的。
en: It's almost as if humour is hard-wired into us.

zh: 所以它肯定有某种好处。
en: So there must be some kind of benefit to it.

zh: 幽默能为我们做什么？
en: What can humour do for us?

zh: 根据最近的研究，幽默具有生理和心理双重效应。
en: According to recent research, humour has both physical and psychological effects.

zh: 让我们来谈谈生理效应。
en: Let's talk about the physical effects.

zh: 首先，笑有助于缓解紧张。
en: Firstly, laughter helps relieve tension.

zh: 它能放松我们身体的肌肉，并且这种效果在一次开怀大笑后能持续长达45分钟。
en: It relaxes the muscles in our body, and the effects can be felt for as long as 45 minutes after a good laugh.

zh: 此外，开怀大笑能增加流向心脏的血液并改善血管功能，这有助于保护我们免受心脏病的侵害。
en: Also, a good laugh increases blood flow to the heart and improves the function of blood vessels, which helps protect us from heart disease.

zh: 最后，当我们笑的时候，体内压力化学物质的水平会下降，而免疫细胞的数量会增加。
en: Finally, when we laugh, the level of stress chemicals in our body falls, and the number of immune cells in our body increases.

zh: 这两者都意味着我们的身体能更好地抵抗感染。
en: Both mean that our body is better able to fight infection.

zh: 有一首歌这样唱道：“微笑吧，即使心已碎。”
en: There is a song that says, "Smile, though your heart is breaking."

zh: 这是否意味着微笑和情绪之间存在联系？
en: Does it mean there is a relation between smiling and mood?

zh: 幽默对我们的心理也有影响。
en: Humour also has psychological effects on our mind.

zh: 科学家已经证明，微笑和情绪之间存在双向效应：当我们快乐时，我们自然会微笑；而当我们微笑时，即使我们并非发自内心，它也能让我们感到快乐。
en: Scientists have proved that there is a two-way effect between smiling and mood: when we're happy, we naturally smile; and when we smile, even if we don't feel like it, it can also make us feel happy.

zh: 此外，当我们将幽默融入我们的生活态度时，它能产生深远的影响。
en: Besides, when we incorporate humour as part of our life attitude, it can have far-reaching effects.

zh: “深远的影响”是什么意思？
en: What does "far-reaching effects" mean?

zh: 它意味着它将对我们的生活方式产生巨大影响。
en: It means that it will have a great influence on how we live.

zh: 我们都知道，如果我们心情不好，我们很可能会表现出消极的态度和情绪；这也会让我们周围的人感到沮丧。
en: We all know that if we are in a bad mood, we are likely to express negative attitudes and feelings; and this makes those around us down, too.

zh: 但如果我们总是努力保持积极和风趣，人们会喜欢和我们在一起，并从中感受到活力。
en: But if we always try to be positive and funny, people will enjoy our company and feel energised by it.

zh: 我的演讲到此结束。
en: That's the end of my talk.

zh: 我希望你们能从生活中的幽默受益。
en: I hope you'll benefit from humour in your lives.

zh: 现在我很乐意回答任何问题......
en: And now I'm happy to answer any questions ...

zh: 但我真的让自己很尴尬。
en: But I really embarrassed myself.

zh: 真的吗？你做了什么？
en: Really? What did you do?

zh: 我一直把主人的名字搞错！
en: I kept on getting the host's name wrong!

zh: 当我们到达那里时，我向别人介绍他叫'理查德'。
en: When we got there, I introduced him to someone as 'Richard'.

zh: 但他的名字是克利夫。
en: But his name is Cliff.

zh: 然后，就在聚会结束时，我走到他面前，当着所有人的面说——'谢谢你举办这么棒的聚会，理查德'。
en: Then, right at the end of the party, I went to him and said — in front of everybody — 'Thanks for the wonderful party, Richard'.

zh: 其他人都变得非常安静。
en: Everybody else got really quiet.

zh: 过了一会儿，有人在我耳边低声说。
en: After a while, somebody whispered in my ear.

zh: '他的名字是克利夫，不是理查德。'
en: 'His name is Cliff, not Richard.'

zh: 你是如何处理这种情况的？
en: How did you handle the situation?

zh: 我拿自己开了个玩笑，这有助于解决所有的尴尬。
en: I made a joke about myself and that helped solve all the embarrassments.

zh: 告诉我更多关于这件事。
en: Tell me more about it.

zh: 嗯，你知道我最近读了一篇文章，介绍了一些解决尴尬情况的方法。
en: Well, you know I'd recently read an article that introduced some ways to solve an awkward situation.

zh: 其中一个例子是，你可以用幽默来让人们取笑你。
en: One example of this is that you can use humour to get people to laugh at you.

zh: 例如，如果你不小心把咖啡洒了自己一身，你可以自嘲或开个玩笑。
en: For instance, if you accidentally pour coffee all over yourself, you can laugh at yourself or make a joke about it.

zh: 例如，你可以说：'我的T恤也想喝杯咖啡。'
en: You can say, for example, 'My T-shirt also wants to have a cup of coffee.'

zh: 这样，其他人也可以笑，而不会因为取笑别人而显得无礼。
en: That way, other people can laugh too, without being rude about laughing at someone else's expense.

zh: 笑声是在尴尬情况下让人们感到舒适的好方法。
en: Laughter is a great way to make people feel comfortable in an awkward situation.

zh: 所以我就开始笑起来，说：'哦天哪，总有一天我会忘记自己的名字！'
en: So I just started laughing and said, 'Oh dear, one of these days I'm going to forget my own name!'

zh: 每个人都笑了，甚至理查德——我是说克利夫。
en: Everybody laughed, even Richard — I mean Cliff.

zh: 幽默确实是处理尴尬情况的好方法。
en: Humour is really a great way to deal with an awkward situation.

zh: 你好，威尔逊教授，感谢您今天加入我们。
en: Hello, Professor Wilson, and thank you for joining us today.

zh: 您能告诉我们一些关于教育的事情吗？
en: Would you please tell us something about education?

zh: 教育具有重大的社会和个人意义；因此，哲学家和教育家花了大量时间探索其意义。
en: Education has great social and personal significance; thus, philosophers and educators have devoted a good deal of time to exploring its meaning.

zh: 那么，他们的一些想法是什么？
en: So, what are some of their thoughts?

zh: 嗯，例如，现代第一位伟大的教育哲学家夸美纽斯坚持认为，教学时，学科内容应适应学习者的兴趣，教学方法应与其心智发展相一致。
en: Well, for example, the first great educational philosopher of modern times, Comenius, insisted that when teaching, the subject matter should be adapted to learners' interests, and the method of instruction should be consistent with their mental development.

zh: 让教育符合学习者的兴趣并满足他们的需求是很重要的。
en: It's important to make education match the learners' interests and meet their needs.

zh: 另一位世界著名的哲学家和教育家约翰·杜威说过：'教育不是为生活做准备；教育就是生活本身。'
en: Another world-famous philosopher and educator, John Dewey, said that 'education is not preparation for life; education is life itself.'

zh: 这到底是什么意思？
en: What does that mean exactly?

zh: 嗯，我认为这意味着教育不仅仅是传递未来生活所需的知识和技能，而是在学习使用这些知识和技能解决现实世界问题的同时，提供丰富的生活体验。
en: Well, I think it means that education is not simply about passing on knowledge and skills necessary for a future life, but to provide a rich experience of living while learning to use that knowledge and those skills to solve real-world problems.

zh: 确实，教育和社会应该紧密相连。
en: Indeed, education and society should be closely connected.

zh: 如今，教育更强调培养创造性思维、探索真理，以及培养对我们同胞的同情和理解。
en: Nowadays, education stresses more on cultivating creative minds, exploring the truth and developing sympathy for and understanding of our fellow men.

zh: 我一直在研究我们学习的方式。
en: I've been looking at ways we learn.

zh: 前几天我在网上看到了一张图表。
en: I saw a diagram on the Internet the other day.

zh: 它显示我们记住了所听到内容的百分之十。
en: It showed that we remember ten percent of what we hear.

zh: 我们记住了所读内容的百分之二十，所看内容的百分之三十，所见所闻的百分之五十，与他人讨论内容的百分之七十，所做内容的百分之八十，以及教给别人内容的百分之九十五。
en: We remember twenty percent of what we read, thirty percent of what we see, fifty percent of what we see and hear, seventy percent of what we discuss with others, eighty percent of what we do and ninety-five percent of what we teach to others.

zh: 这与我被告知的情况相符，即如果你想确定自己理解了某件事，那就试着把它教给别人。
en: It ties in with what I've been told, that if you want to be sure you understand something then try and teach it to someone else.

zh: 如果你无法解释它，那么你自己就没有清楚地理解它。
en: If you can't explain it, then you don't understand it clearly yourself.

zh: 还记得我们老师前几天告诉我们的关于我们如何学习的话吗？
en: Remember what our teacher told us the other day about how we learn?

zh: 它是按这个顺序进行的：首先我们记住，然后我们理解，然后我们应用所学到的东西。
en: It goes in this order: first we remember, then we understand, then we apply what we have learnt.

zh: 之后我们分析并评估发生的事情，然后从我们学到的东西中创造出新的东西。
en: After that we analyse and evaluate what happened and then create something new from what we have learnt.

zh: 让我们试一试！
en: Let's give it a try!

zh: 在今天的讲座中，我们将讨论教育。
en: In today's lecture, we'll be discussing education.

zh: 我们经常问的关于教育的问题是：什么是教育？
en: The questions we often ask about education are: what is education?

zh: 学生接受教育的主要目标是什么？
en: And what are the main objectives for students to receive education?

zh: 教育一词来源于拉丁语，意思是“引导”。
en: Education comes from the Latin for "to lead".

zh: 所以，当你接受教育时，你被从一个地方引导到另一个地方，包括身体上、道德上、精神上或学术上。
en: So, when you are educated you are led from one place to another, including physical, moral, mental or academic.

zh: 它是一个促进学习或获取知识、技能、价值观、态度和习惯的过程。
en: It is a process of facilitating learning or the acquisition of knowledge, skills, values, attitudes and habits.

zh: 让我们继续谈谈教育的目标。
en: Let's move on to talk about the objectives of education.

zh: 对我来说，教育有三个主要目标。
en: For me, education has three main objectives.

zh: 首先，是为了获取知识。
en: Firstly, it is to gain knowledge.

zh: 显然，某些知识不仅是追求某些职业所必需的，而且也是为世界做贡献所必需的。
en: Obviously, certain kinds of knowledge are necessary not only to pursue certain careers but also to contribute to the world.

zh: 例如，我们需要学习数学来理解金融和经济；我们需要能够阅读，这样我们才能够沟通并接触新事物。
en: For example, we need to learn maths to understand finance and economics; we need to be able to read, so that we are able to communicate and expose ourselves to new things.

zh: 此外，教育提供了另一种更深层次的知识，它让我们理解周围世界是如何运作的。
en: Besides, education provides another deeper kind of knowledge, which lets us understand how the surrounding world works.

zh: 它让我们洞察人们如何思考，以及我们需要什么样的态度来理解不同文化的人。
en: It gives us insights into how people think and the attitude we need in order to understand people of different cultures.

zh: 其次，教育超越知识；它为个人在社会中的全面发展做准备。
en: Secondly, education goes beyond knowledge; it prepares individuals for a well-rounded life in society.

zh: 换句话说，它为学习者提供了发展技能、独立思考以及终身持续学习的工具。
en: In other words, it provides learners with the tools to develop skills, to think for themselves and to continue learning throughout their lives.

zh: 例如，教育可以帮助个人发展批判性思维能力，这将防止他们不加批判地遵循传统方式，并使他们能够基于判断行事。
en: For example, education can help individuals develop critical thinking skills, which will prevent them from following uncritically traditional ways and will enable them to act based on judgement.

zh: 第三，教育建立核心价值观，并为你适应社会生活做准备。
en: Thirdly, education establishes core values and prepares you for social life.

zh: 它帮助你理解对与错，探索真理并发展认知，无论其是否容易或他人的意见如何；它还帮助你学会如何以文明的方式与他人互动，无论国籍、种族或阶级。
en: It helps you to understand what is wrong and what is right, explore the truth and develop cognition, regardless of whether it's easy or what others' opinions are; it also helps you learn how to interact with other people in a civil manner, regardless of nationality, race or class.

zh: 它使人们行为正直，无论背景和环境如何。
en: It makes people behave with integrity, regardless of background and circumstances.

zh: 因此，社会中将会出现更多批判、善良、诚实和真实的公民。
en: Therefore, there will be more critical, kind, honest and truthful citizens in society.

zh: 最终，教育也使学习者能够树立正确的价值观和态度，并形成良好习惯，从而也能成为负责任的公民。
en: Eventually, education also enables learners to establish the right values and attitudes, as well as form good habits so that they can become responsible citizens as well.

zh: 总之，教育之所以重要，不仅因为它促进个人发展，还因为它促进社会和国家的进步。
en: In conclusion, education is important not only because it facilitates individual development, but also promotes social and national development.

zh: 回顾最初提供的定义，教育促进个人在身体、心理、道德和学术上的发展，从而使个人能够为社会的发展做出有效贡献。
en: Referring back to the definition provided at the very beginning, education facilitates the development of an individual physically, mentally, morally and academically, so that the individual can make effective contributions to social development.

zh: 詹姆斯教授，非常感谢您非常有见地的演讲。
en: Professor James, thank you very much for the very insightful talk.

zh: 它让我重新思考教育的目标。
en: It makes me rethink the objectives of education.

zh: 我的问题是：为了实现教育的主要目标，教师和学生应该分别做什么？
en: My question is: in order to achieve the main objectives of education, what should teachers and students do respectively?

zh: 这是一个非常好的问题。
en: That is a very good question.

zh: 让我们首先谈谈教师的责任。
en: Let's first talk about teachers' responsibility.

zh: 我认为教师的首要任务是促进学生的学习。
en: I think a teacher's top priority is to facilitate students' learning.

zh: 这意味着教师的工作不仅仅是向学生传授知识和技能，更重要的是激发学生的学习兴趣，并教会他们批判性和创造性地思考。
en: That means a teacher's job is not just to pass on knowledge and skills to students, but it's more important to stimulate students' interests in learning and teach them to think critically and creatively.

zh: 此外，教师需要通过提供丰富的资源并帮助他们学会使用正确的策略来支持学生的学习，使学习更有效。
en: Also, a teacher needs to support students' learning by providing rich resources and helping them learn to use the right strategies to make learning more effective.

zh: 那学生的责任呢？
en: What about the students' responsibility?

zh: 嗯，我相信学生的责任是主动成为自主学习者。
en: Well, I believe it is the students' responsibility to take the initiative to become autonomous learners.

zh: 这就是为什么学生需要为自己设定明确的学习目标，并学会如何分析、比较和评价所教的内容。
en: That's why students need to have clear learning goals for themselves and to learn how to analyse, compare and evaluate what is taught.

zh: 对学生来说另一件重要的事情是，他们需要做出正确的判断来指导自己的信念和行动，并且在被评估时展示所学是一个绝妙的主意。
en: Another thing that's important for students is that they need to make proper judgements to guide their own beliefs and actions, and it's a brilliant idea to demonstrate what they have learnt when assessed.

zh: 嗨，马克，谈到媒体，比如电视、广播、互联网或其他类型的社交媒体，你有最喜欢的媒体类型吗？
en: Hi Mark, talking about media, such as TV radio, the Internet, or other types of social media, do you have a favourite type of media?

zh: 老实说，我每天都会使用很多媒体。
en: To be honest, I use so much media on a daily basis.

zh: 我喜欢早上听广播；下午，我通过互联网了解新闻。
en: I like listening to broadcasts on the radio in the mornings; and in the afternoons, I catch up on news via the Internet.

zh: 晚上，我喜欢和家人一起看电视或电影。
en: In the evenings, I enjoy watching TV or films with my family.

zh: 所以我真的没有最喜欢的类型。
en: So I don't really have a favourite type.

zh: 看来你在不同时间选择不同的媒体。
en: It seems that you choose different media at different time.

zh: 广播可以帮助人们快速了解交通信息、天气状况、新闻广播、经济或政治讨论以及最新的音乐。
en: The radio helps give people quick news about traffic information, weather conditions, news broadcasts, economic or political discussions and the latest music available.

zh: 另一方面，互联网为我们提供了关于世界的如此多样的信息，从艺术和文学到科学。
en: On the other hand, the Internet gives us so much diverse information about the world, from art and literature to science.

zh: 你认为媒体是积极的还是消极的？
en: Do you think media is a positive or a negative thing?

zh: 嗯，这很难说——它是两者的混合体。
en: Well, that's difficult to say — it's a mixture of both.

zh: 媒体既有优点也有缺点。
en: There are both advantages and disadvantages to the media.

zh: 例如，一个缺点是信息太多样化了；因此，很难找到正确的信息并决定信息是否可信。
en: For example, one disadvantage is that there is so much diverse information available; hence it can be difficult to find the right information and to decide if the information can be trusted.

zh: 弄清楚该承认什么和该摒弃什么真的很有挑战性。
en: Figuring out what to acknowledge and what to dismiss is really challenging.

zh: 因此，如何解释和评估我们从媒体获得的信息对我们所有人都至关重要。
en: Therefore, how to interpret and evaluate information we get from the media can be critical for all of us.

zh: 那么，媒体有什么优点吗？
en: So, are there any advantages to media?

zh: 媒体让我们了解世界上正在发生的事情，它也可以概述社会中的问题，这样我们就可以共同努力找到解决这些问题的方法。
en: Media allows us to know what's going on in the world, and it can also outline problems in society, so we can work together to find ways to solve them.

zh: 我以前没有那样想过媒体，谢谢你，马克！
en: I had not thought of media that way, thanks Mark!

zh: 本，你经常使用互联网吗？
en: Ben, do you use the Internet often?

zh: 你用它来做什么？
en: What do you use it for?

zh: 我每天都使用互联网！
en: I use the Internet every single day!

zh: 它是一种非常有用的媒体。
en: It's a very useful piece of media.

zh: 我查阅互联网来为学校研究东西，通过寻找关于该主题的不同意见来更好地理解复杂的问题，甚至做一些有趣的事情，比如观看乒乓球锦标赛。
en: I consult the Internet to research things for school, to better understand complicated issues by finding alternative opinions on the subject and even to do fun things like watching Ping-Pong tournaments.

zh: 互联网真的很有用。
en: The Internet is really useful.

zh: 我喜欢使用它，但我不像你那样经常使用它。
en: I enjoy using it, but I don't use it as often as you seem to.

zh: 我仍然更喜欢使用传统媒体，如报纸、杂志和广播。
en: I still prefer to use traditional media like newspapers, magazines and the radio.

zh: 嗯，你知道你现在可以在互联网上找到你提到的所有东西吗？
en: Well, did you know that you can find all the things you mentioned on the Internet now?

zh: 我在我最喜欢的报纸网站上阅读新闻，而不是购买实体报纸，我还可以购买电子杂志——你可以在线阅读的杂志。
en: I read the news on my favourite newspaper's website, instead of buying a real newspaper, and I can also purchase e-magazines — magazines you can read online.

zh: 如今你甚至可以在互联网上收听你最喜欢的广播电台。
en: You can even listen to your favourite radio station on the Internet these days.

zh: 互联网能提供如此多不同类型的媒体，真是太神奇了！
en: It's amazing that the Internet can provide so many different types of media!

zh: 现在是《请教专家》节目时间。
en: And now it's time for Ask the Expert.

zh: 我们本周的专家是杰索普教授，一位媒体研究教授。
en: Our expert this week is Professor Jessop, a professor of media studies.

zh: 很高兴您能参加我们的节目，杰索普教授。
en: Good to have you on our programme, Professor Jessop.

zh: 我从我们的网站上收集了一些问题。
en: I have collected a number of questions from our website.

zh: 这是第一个问题。
en: Here is the first question.

zh: 你认为互联网影响了传统媒体吗，例如新闻？
en: Do you think the Internet has affected traditional media, news, for example?

zh: 互联网对新闻的制作方式产生了巨大影响。
en: The Internet has made a great impact on how news is produced.

zh: 过去，是由报纸编辑决定什么是新闻并进行报道，但现在任何人都可以。
en: In the past, it was the newspaper editor who decided what was news and covered it, but now anyone can.

zh: 利用我们智能手机中的摄像头，我们可以录制一个事件的高清视频，将其上传到互联网，然后全世界的人都能看到。
en: With the camera in our smartphones, we can make a recording of a high-definition video of an event, upload it to the Internet, and then people all over the world can see it.

zh: 结果，大型媒体公司被排除在外了。
en: As a result, big media corporations are being cut out.

zh: 一些网站直接从正在经历或目睹事件的人那里获取新闻，例如在车祸现场。
en: Some sites are bringing the news directly from the people who are experiencing or witnessing it, on the spot of a car accident for example.

zh: 杰索普教授，刚才您提到了互联网如何改变了新闻，我们的第二个问题是：它是如何改变我们使用语言的方式的？
en: Just now, you mentioned how the Internet has changed news, Professor Jessop, our second question is: how has it changed the way we use language?

zh: 嗯，显然互联网给我们的语言带来了很多新词。
en: Well, obviously the Internet has introduced a lot of new words to our language.

zh: 在互联网出现之前，谁听说过“微博”呢？
en: Who had heard of "microblogging" before the Internet?

zh: 但更重要的是，我相信互联网使媒体更具互动性。
en: But more importantly, I believe the Internet has made media more interactive.

zh: 媒体过去是一个单向的过程——他们说，我们听。
en: Media used to be a one-way process — they spoke and we listened.

zh: 现在则更像是一场对话。
en: Now it's much more like a dialogue.

zh: 每篇文章下面都有读者的评论，任何人都可以表达他们的想法。
en: Every article has reader's comments below it and anyone can express what they think.

zh: 现在我们只剩下时间再问一个问题了。
en: Now we've only got time for one more question.

zh: 它们现在处境艰难。
en: They're having a hard time.

zh: 您认为我们未来还会读报纸吗？
en: Do you think we will still read newspapers in the future?

zh: 在过去的几年里，大多数国家的报纸销量都出现了下降。
en: Most countries have seen a drop in newspaper sales in the last few years.

zh: 不仅仅是读者的流失是个问题。
en: It's not just the loss of readers that is a problem.

zh: 在美国，报纸几乎90%的收入来自广告。
en: In the US, newspapers make almost 90% of their money from advertising.

zh: 这有几个原因。
en: This has occurred for several reasons.

zh: 首先，现在很多公司和个人选择在线做广告。
en: Firstly, a lot of companies and people now choose to advertise online.

zh: 其次，当报纸销量下降时，想在报纸上做广告的人就更少，他们也不愿意支付那么多广告费。
en: Secondly, when newspaper sales drop, fewer people want to advertise in the paper and they won't pay as much to advertise.

zh: 在我看来，报业未来会发现生存更加困难。
en: In my opinion, newspaper business will find it much harder to survive in the future.

zh: 和您交谈总是很愉快，感谢您参加《请教专家》。
en: It's a pleasure as always to talk to you and thanks for taking part in Ask the Expert.

zh: 大约30年前，每个人都看同样的新闻，并在第二天工作时谈论它。
en: About 30 years ago, everyone watched the same news and talked about it at work the next day.

zh: 哦？这和我们现在的做法很不一样。
en: Oh? That is very different from what we do nowadays.

zh: 在那些日子里，家家户户都会在电视上看晚间新闻。
en: In those days, the families would watch the evening news on TV.

zh: 那么这是不是意味着每个人都看同一个电视节目？
en: So does that mean that everyone watched the same TV programme?

zh: 你能告诉我更多关于它的事吗？
en: Can you tell me more about it?

zh: 晚饭后，家人们会围坐在电视机旁，从晚上7点到7点半看新闻。
en: After dinner, families would gather around the television and watch the news from 7 pm to 7.30 pm.

zh: 但我不太明白的是，家里的每个人都一起看同样的新闻。
en: But what I don't quite understand is that everyone in the family watched the same news together.

zh: 他们都有相同的兴趣吗？
en: Did they all have the same interests?

zh: 好吧，让我换一种说法。
en: Well, let me put it another way.

zh: 由于当时获取最新消息的途径有限，看晚间新闻成了许多家庭的一种普遍做法。
en: Due to the limited ways to find out the most up-to-date news in those days, watching the evening news became a kind of common practice for many families.

zh: 我说清楚了吗？
en: Did I make myself clearer?

zh: 啊，那一定是在我们有电脑和互联网之前发生的。
en: Ah, that must have happened before we had computers and the Internet.

zh: 我们现在可以一天24小时随时关注新闻。
en: We now can follow the news at any time 24 hours a day.
